title: Orion Therapeutics: SIG/CAIQ intake and evidence handoff
preview:
From: Claire Dawson <claire.dawson@orion-thera.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Allen Park <allen.park@orion-thera.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG/CAIQ intake - Orion Therapeutics

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We're starting the SIG/CAIQ intake for a new procurement track and would like to schedule an intake call to align on scope and evidence handoff. High-level asks from procurement/security are:

- Confirm which CAIQ sections Redwood will answer vs. which we need screenshots/documents for.
- Examples of audit logs (sampling) and retention policy language.
- Clarify key management: KMS/HSM usage and whether customer-managed keys are supported.
- Data residency options and where logs/indices are stored.

Are you available for a 30–45 minute call this week? We prefer Wed/Thu mornings PT. I attached a short doc with our CAIQ priority sections to help focus the conversation.

Thanks,

Claire Dawson
Security Lead, Orion Therapeutics
claire.dawson@orion-thera.com
(415) 555-0119

Attachment: orion_caiq_priorities.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Claire Dawson <claire.dawson@orion-thera.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ intake - Orion Therapeutics

Hi Claire — thanks for the note and the priorities doc. Wed/Thu mornings PT work for us. Proposed slots:

- Wed 2026-06-10 09:30–10:00 PT
- Wed 2026-06-10 10:30–11:00 PT
- Thu 2026-06-11 09:00–09:45 PT

I'll block the first feasible slot and we can adjust.

Quick pre-call context to accelerate the intake:
- I've attached a one-pager SOC 2 summary that maps to common SIG/CAIQ questions (redwood_soc2_summary.pdf).
- For the call, Aditya (security engineering) will cover logs/KMS/certificates and Marissa (compliance ops) will cover evidence packaging and timelines.
- If there are specific artifacts you need on the call (sample logs, policy language, network diagrams), list them now and we'll bring samples into the folder before the meeting.

Proposed agenda (30–45m):
1) Introductions & scope confirmation (5m)
2) CAIQ section priorities (10m)
3) Evidence handoff plan & owners (10–15m)
4) Timeline and next steps (5m)

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference
Attachment: redwood_soc2_summary.pdf

From: Claire Dawson <claire.dawson@orion-thera.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Allen Park <allen.park@orion-thera.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ intake - Orion Therapeutics

Ben — thanks, Thu at 09:00 PT works for us. Confirmed: Thu 2026-06-11 09:00 PT (calendar invite to follow).

On the artifacts/examples we'd like to see on the call and in the drive ahead of time:
- Sample audit log export (redacted) showing user IDs, timestamps, and API keys usage over a week.
- Snapshot of access control configuration for admin vs. app roles.
- Encryption at rest: proof of disk encryption and KMS usage (CMK vs. RMK) and whether customer-managed keys are supported.
- Retention policy excerpt and an example deletion request flow.

I've attached a small PDF called \"CAIQ priorities\" which highlights the specific CAIQ questions our vendor risk team will prioritize. If you can pre-populate a few CAIQ items and upload them to a shared folder before the call, that would be ideal.

Claire
Claire Dawson
Security Lead, Orion Therapeutics
Attachment: orion_caiq_priorities.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Claire Dawson <claire.dawson@orion-thera.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.inference>, Allen Park <allen.park@orion-thera.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ intake - Orion Therapeutics

Hi Claire / team — great call this morning, thanks for the clear priorities. Meeting notes and next actions below. I'm adding a short handoff timeline so procurement has visibility.

--- Meeting notes (2026-06-11) ---
Attendees: Claire Dawson (Orion), Allen Park (Orion), Ben Carter (Redwood), Aditya Rao (Redwood), Marissa Cole (Redwood)

Key clarifications
- CAIQ split: Redwood will propose answers for ~80% of the technical CAIQ items; items requiring Orion-owned screenshots (e.g., your tenant config) will be flagged and clearly documented.
- Logs: Redwood can provide a weekly sample redacted log export (JSON/CSV) showing user_id, timestamp, request_path, response_code. Aditya to upload a 7-day sample to the shared Drive.
- KMS: Redwood uses AWS KMS for default keys; we support BYOK via customer-managed KMS with the Dedicated/Private tiers. We do not offer HSM appliance handover in Hosted mode.
- Data residency: Redwood hosts customer data in region-specific clusters; logs for hosted customers are stored in the same region unless otherwise contracted.

Action items and owners
1) Aditya Rao — upload redacted 7-day audit log sample to Drive (target: 2026-06-14)
2) Marissa Cole — populate CAIQ draft answers for prioritized sections and share as spreadsheet (target: 2026-06-18)
3) Ben Carter — provide SOC2 one-pager and network diagram samples into intake folder (done)
4) Claire Dawson / Orion — flag any CAIQ rows that require tenant screenshots or signed attestations (target: 2026-06-20)

Deliverables and timeline (high level)
- 2026-06-14: audit log sample uploaded
- 2026-06-18: Redwood CAIQ draft uploaded
- 2026-06-21: joint review call to go over draft answers (if needed)

Shared folder & links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B3cRedwood-Orion-Intake
HubSpot intake record: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/OrionThera/record/OR-45921

Attachments uploaded: caqi_answers_draft.xlsx

If that timeline looks good, we'll move forward and Marissa will ping when the draft is ready for review. Thanks again — please reply if you'd like anything added to the notes.

Best,

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference
(650) 555-0172

title: POC Kickoff - hands-on demo (routing & load plan) — scheduling + prep
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Reyes <sofia.reyes@pikeanalytics.com>, Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@pikeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC Kickoff - hands-on demo (routing & load plan) — scheduling + prep

Hi Sofia / Tom —

Thanks again for committing time for the Pike Analytics POC. We want to run a hands-on session focused on:
- prompt engineering / prompt review for your top 3 workflows
- routing & fallbacks for latency-sensitive queries
- scaled load validation (sample traffic and ramp plan)

Proposed session windows (PT):
- Wednesday 10 Jun, 9:00–11:00am PT — interactive demo + short load rehearsal
- Thursday 11 Jun, 1:00–3:00pm PT — deeper routing review + controlled load tests

Can you confirm which window works best? If neither, suggest two alternatives and we’ll re-run scheduling. Before the call, could your infra lead run a quick connectivity check (we need outbound access to the following endpoints): api.redwoodinference.com:443 and s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com (for model artifacts). I’ll send a one-page network checklist in my next email.

Agenda (draft):
1) 10 min - introductions and success criteria
2) 25 min - live prompt review (you drive examples)
3) 35 min - routing architecture and model variants discussion
4) 30 min - load ramp plan and verification steps
5) 20 min - actions & next steps

Thanks — looking forward.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: (none in this message)


From: Sofia Reyes <sofia.reyes@pikeanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@pikeanalytics.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC Kickoff - hands-on demo (routing & load plan) — scheduling + prep

Hi Ben —

Thanks — Thursday 11 Jun, 1:00–3:00pm PT works best for us (Tom and I are out of the office on the 10th).

Two quick asks before we lock the slot:
- Can you confirm an expected sustained RPS we should plan for during the "controlled load tests"? We want to make sure we allocate capacity.
- Please send the list of IPs and endpoints we need to allow through our firewall (we have strict egress rules).

Also looping in Tom (infra) to coordinate network checks — Tom, can you confirm if 11 Jun 1pm PT is ok for you?

On Mon, Jun 8, 2026 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for committing time for the Pike Analytics POC. We want to run a hands-on session focused on...
>
> Proposed session windows (PT):
> - Wednesday 10 Jun, 9:00–11:00am PT — interactive demo + short load rehearsal
> - Thursday 11 Jun, 1:00–3:00pm PT — deeper routing review + controlled load tests

Best,
Sofia
Sofia Reyes | Head of Data Products
Pike Analytics


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Reyes <sofia.reyes@pikeanalytics.com>, Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@pikeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 13:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC Kickoff - hands-on demo (routing & load plan) — scheduling + prep

Perfect — we’ll lock Thursday 11 Jun, 1:00–3:00pm PT.

Quick answers:
- Controlled load: plan for a stepped ramp to ~150 RPS peak for 20 minutes, then a 30-minute steady-state window at ~80 RPS. That mirrors the throughput profile you mentioned during discovery. We’ll start lower and control the ramp so you can watch failure modes.
- Endpoints to allow (egress): api.redwoodinference.com:443, s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com (for model artifacts), metrics.redwoodinference.com:443 (for optional observability webhook). I’ve attached a short network checklist.

Attachments:
- poc_runbook_v1.pdf (11 pages) — walkthrough + run commands + rollback steps
- network_checklist.txt — minimal firewall entries and test commands

See you on Thu. I’ll ping again tomorrow with a final run order.

Thanks,
Ben

---
Attachment stubs: poc_runbook_v1.pdf (11 pages, runbook), network_checklist.txt (3 lines)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 17:08:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: Pre-call checklist for Pike Analytics POC (Thu 11 Jun, 1pm PT)

Team — quick internal sync before tomorrow's hands-on: please treat as action-oriented.

1) Marissa — lead the demo intro and keep success criteria visible. Prepare the 2-slide "SLOs we will validate" doc (due Thu 9am PT).
2) Dylan — bring the prompt set + routing playbook. I need one sample prompt per workflow by Thu 10am PT so we can load them into the sandbox. Confirm you’ll enable KV cache for the demo.
3) Stephanie — security quick review: confirm we can show audit logs for the session and that the HIPAA toggle is off for demo mode. If there are redlines, flag them immediately.
4) Ben (me) — facilitator, will run load steps and talk through the rollback plan. I’ll own the post-session notes and action tracker.

Checklist reminders:
- Verify artifacts are in S3 and accessible (network_checklist.txt)
- Confirm instrumentation endpoints for latency metrics are reachable
- Prepare a short fallback script if model variant A degrades under load

If anything critical comes up, reply-all and we’ll triage.

Ben


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Reyes <sofia.reyes@pikeanalytics.com>, Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@pikeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: RE: POC Kickoff - hands-on demo (routing & load plan) — recap & next steps

Hi Sofia / Tom —

Great session today — appreciate you carving out the time. Quick recap and owners (short version):

What we ran:
- Prompt review for three production workflows (checkout assistant, billing summarization, account lookup) — tuned two prompts live
- Routing review — validated fallback path to smaller quantized model at 120ms median latency
- Controlled load test — 3-step ramp to 150 RPS peak, 30-minute steady window at 80 RPS; observed 0.3% elevated tail latency during step 2, traced to a VM hot-spot we throttled successfully

Action items (owner — due):
- Dylan Brooks — export the final prompt set and add comments for the two tuned prompts (2026-06-15)
- Marissa Cole — update the SLO doc with measured steady-state latency and share with Pike (2026-06-12)
- Stephanie Nguyen — compile the audit/log evidence for the demo and send the package (post_session_notes.pdf) (2026-06-14)
- Ben Carter — schedule follow-up deep-dive to cover custom reranking and A/B pathing; propose 1.5 hour slot next week (Calendly link below) (2026-06-12)

Notes/asks for Pike Analytics:
- Tom, can you confirm the firewall entries were stable during the load window and share any spikes from your side?
- Sofia, please confirm whether you want us to include the quantized variant as warm fallback in production (we estimated a ~18% unit-cost reduction).

Attachments:
- post_session_notes.pdf (5 pages, includes timeline, logs pointers, and action tracker)

Next steps: please pick a time on my Calendly for the follow-up deep-dive: https://calendly.com/ben-carter/poc-followup. I’ll include an extended runbook and an A/B plan.

Thanks again — nice collaboration.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

---
Recorded snippet and slides uploaded to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-example

Ben

title: Prod inference: startup latency & streaming ops
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Nguyen <alex@finlytics.com>, procurement@finlytics.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 9 Jun 2026 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Prod inference: startup latency & streaming ops

Alex — great conversation this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so we can size a short pilot:  

TL;DR: FinLytics wants the user-visible startup latency to be competitive with current in-house infra; key asks below. We can run a 2-week pilot on Redwood Dedicated with an agreed SLA.

Call notes / asks:
- Desired \"initial byte\" / time-to-first-token (TTFT) targets: p95 <= 300ms for typical prompts (0.5k–2k tokens), p99 target <= 600ms.
- Streaming: web front-end will use SSE; mobile app prefers WebSocket. Customers expect chunk cadence to feel \"live\" — target 200–350ms between chunks for mid-length outputs.
- Concurrency & throughput: expected peak concurrent sessions ~180–220, average sustained requests ~12–20 req/s, per-user concurrency ~2–3.
- Payload profile: median prompt ~650 tokens, 95th ~1.8k tokens, model outputs typically 300–1k tokens.
- Fallback behavior: if we miss TTFT SLO, automatic fallback to smaller quantized variant is acceptable as long as semantics are preserved.
- Observability: they want p95/p99 TTFT and chunk cadence, plus per-route token cost breakdown in the console.

Attachments sent: redwood-slo-playbook.pdf (SLO definitions and pilot runbook). Can you confirm the target percentile numbers above are what you need us to validate? Also please attach a small sample prompt set and any non-functional constraints (e.g., browser environments, mobile SDK limitations).

Next steps proposed:
1) Customer to share sample prompts and any procurement / security checklist (procurement@finlytics.com).
2) Schedule a 60-minute technical deep-dive with our infra team (I propose Thu 11 Jun, 10:00 PT).
3) Redwood to provide a pilot proposal and a short runbook (attached) with metrics we will measure.

Thanks — I’ll loop in Marissa and Grace to scope the pilot.

—
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachments: redwood-slo-playbook.pdf


From: Alex Nguyen <alex@finlytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finlytics.com
Date: Wed, 9 Jun 2026 17:35:12 -0400
Subject: Re: Prod inference: startup latency & streaming ops

Thanks Ben — good summary. Confirming targets from product side and adding a few clarifications:  

Confirmed SLOs:
- Initial byte / TTFT: p95 <= 300ms for prompts up to ~2k tokens; p99 target <= 600ms.
- Streaming chunk cadence: target 200–350ms between chunks for the UI to feel responsive; acceptable up to 500ms at very high load but that should be flagged.
- Per-user concurrency: 2 concurrent requests; peak concurrent sessions in US region ~200.
- Sustained throughput: baseline 12 req/s, bursting up to 22 req/s for short periods during business hours.

Additional notes:
- Mobile app: we prefer WebSocket for background/resume behavior on iOS/Android. Web (desktop) should use SSE where possible.
- Security/compliance: procurement will push over our vendor security questionnaire after we agree on pilot scope.
- Pilot dataset: attaching a 40–60 prompt sample set and a short note on expected completions.

We’re aiming for a two-week technical pilot. For success criteria we want: TTFT p95 within target for 75% of test prompts during busy windows, chunk cadence <= 350ms for 80% of streamed responses, and no more than one incident requiring a manual rollback during the pilot.

Attaching: finlytics-sample-prompts.json

Let me know if Thu 11 Jun 10:00 PT works for the deep-dive.

—
Alex Nguyen
Head of Product | FinLytics
alex@finlytics.com | +1 (312) 555-0199

- Trade-offs doc for SSE vs WebSocket and recommended chunk sizes and pacing knobs.
- A draft pilot runbook with test harness, observability checks, and rollback criteria.

Marissa / Grace — can you lead the technical deep-dive? Specific asks for the call:
- Marissa: bring the pilot SLO template and rough cost estimate for a 2-week Dedicated pilot (include burst pool sizing).

Alex — attached is the pilot-runbook draft. If procurement needs anything ahead of the call, loop them into our security checklist (we’ll follow up with SOC 2 evidence if required).

title: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>, security@heliospay.com
Cc: audits@prisma-audits.com
Date: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Team — HeliosPay kicked off an initial SIG/SOC2 checklist review and asked for a set of small, targeted architecture vignettes that show how we isolate inference workloads in two operator modes: 1) Redwood Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) and 2) Redwood Private (customer VPC with on‑prem connector).

They specifically asked for: 
- One-page diagram per vignette (PDF SVG preferred) with annotated data flows and egress controls.
- A short matrix mapping each diagram to SIG controls (segmentation, tenancy, control plane separation) — 1 tab in an XLSX is fine.
- Callouts for KMS/HSM boundaries, logging/audit emission points, and fallback behavior when Dedicated capacity is saturated.

Deliverables I want us to hand to HeliosPay by end of next week (EOD Fri): sketches-v1.pdf (attached), isolation-matrix.xlsx (attached) + a single summary email to their security contact.

Pointers for the vignettes: 
- Dedicated vignette: show tenant-tagged inference queue -> reserved GPU pool -> prefix/KV cache layer -> outbound egress only via customer-approved NAT or private peering. Indicate service mesh control-plane traffic separation.
- Private vignette: show customer VPC with VPC‑peering or dedicated interconnect, sidecar-run inference nodes (optional on-prem gateway), and airgap/bridge option for regulated customers.

Kimberly — can you draw the diagrams and export to PDF + SVG? Rishi — can you produce the SIG mapping sheet and short language for the auditor? Marissa — please own the HeliosPay point of contact communication and signoff. Logan — I'll loop you in if we need to run the sketches by Solutions Eng for implementation nuance.

I attached an early whiteboard export (sketches-v1.pdf) with very rough boxes — use it only as a starting point; avoid copying the wording verbatim for the auditor.

Thanks — short meeting ok today at 11:00 if anyone wants to do a 20-minute alignment.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwoodinference.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0112

Attachments: sketches-v1.pdf (1.2MB)

title: Re: Documentation request — minors' data & DPIA evidence
preview:
From: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@acmekids.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Kids Apps Legal <legal@acmekids.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Minors' data handling: COPPA / DPIA confirmation needed

Hi Ben,

We're doing a quick compliance sign-off ahead of a feature roll-out where Acme Kids Apps will surface personalized recommendations to our users. As part of our vendor intake we need written confirmation and some evidence from Redwood on the following items: 

- A short attestation that Redwood will not knowingly accept or process personal data from children under 13 (COPPA definition) unless we have parental consent and product policies in place.
- A high-level DPIA summary or the sections relevant to processing of children’s data (if any).
- Confirmation whether Redwood has any model training or dataset ingestion that includes children’s personal data (internal or customer-provided).
- Age-gating / consent tooling guidance you provide to customers and any recommended configuration for SDKs.
- The current Data Processing Addendum (DPA) including SCCs and whether Redwood can sign a clause limiting processing of minors' data.
- If available, a completed SIG/CAIQ or SOC 2 evidence to speed our review.

Can you provide a written attestation and the DPIA summary by end of week (Friday, 6/11)? If you prefer, happy to jump on a short call. We've attached our vendor intake questionnaire (see attached).

Thanks,
Tom
Tom Reynolds
Head of Privacy & Compliance
Acme Kids Apps

Attachment: vendor_questionnaire_acme.xlsx (questionnaire stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@acmekids.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Minors' data handling: COPPA / DPIA confirmation needed

Tom —

Thanks for sending this over and for the clear checklist. Looping in Lauren (Legal) and Evelyn (Privacy) to provide the attestation and DPIA material.

Quick notes: we can provide a short customer-facing attestation and a DPIA summary document. Evelyn will confirm technical controls and whether any training datasets contain children’s data. Lauren will review the DPA/SCC question and can propose contract language.

I'll aim to get a first package to you by Thursday EOD and the attestation text for signature by Friday. If you'd like a 30-minute sync to walk the materials, I can book time — otherwise we'll send the docs via email and a DocuSign link for any DPA redline.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

Attachment: redwood_coppa_cust_facing_statement.pdf (stub)

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@acmekids.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 14:45:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Minors' data handling: COPPA / DPIA confirmation needed

Hi Tom / Ben —

Thanks, Tom — a few clarifying points up front and a short summary of controls we can share.

Clarifying questions:
- Will Acme be sending any PII that explicitly identifies users as under 13 (e.g., birthdate field or "is_minor" flag)?
- Are you expecting Redwood to host or persist any parental consent artifacts?

Controls and current posture (summary):
- Policy: Our Terms of Service and Acceptable Use Guidelines prohibit intentional targeting of minors under 13 in downstream applications and we require customers to represent they have lawful basis for any personal data supplied.
- Training data: Redwood's hosted models are not trained on customer-provided inputs by default. Customers can opt in to allow their data to be used for model improvement; that is a separate contractual opt-in and we do not enable it for customers processing children’s data without documented parental consent and additional safeguards.
- Age-gating guidance: we provide SDK flags and recommended best-practice flows (client-side age gate + parental consent server flow). We can share snippet code and a recommended config for tokenization/retention.
- Data deletion and retention: We expose a standard deletion endpoint for customer records (API + support workflows) and offer configurable retention windows. Logs that could include PII are subject to automatic redaction and limited retention.
- Security: We support customer KMS/HSM integrations for encryption-at-rest, TLS in transit, role-based access, and audit logs (can provide truncated audit evidence).

Attachments I've prepared for your intake:
- dpia_outline_2026-06.pdf (DPIA summary focused on processing risk & mitigation)
- data_flow_diagram.png (high-level flow showing where customer data enters, is cached, and retained)

I'll mark items that need Legal sign-off (DPA wording around minors) so Lauren can pick up. If Acme can share whether they will send birthdate/age flags that would help finalize the attestation wording.

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Privacy & Compliance Manager — Redwood Inference
evelyn.hart@redwood.com

Attachments: dpia_outline_2026-06.pdf, data_flow_diagram.png

From: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@acmekids.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Kids Apps Legal <legal@acmekids.com>
Date: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 08:22:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Minors' data handling: COPPA / DPIA confirmation needed

Thanks Evelyn — that's helpful. A couple follow-ups:

1) For contracting, we'd want a simple attestation clause similar to: "Vendor represents and warrants that it will not knowingly solicit, collect, or process personal data of children under 13 on behalf of Customer unless (i) Customer has obtained verifiable parental consent, and (ii) Vendor has been notified and agreed to processing under a documented annex." Are you able to accept that language or propose minor edits?

2) Can you provide any of the following evidence artifacts to accelerate procurement: a completed CAIQ or SIG Lite, SOC 2 report (redacted), or a DPA with SCCs attached?

3) Target timeline: we need the attestation and DPIA summary for our compliance team by Friday (6/11). If you can't meet that exact date please let us know what is feasible.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Tom

--
Tom Reynolds
Head of Privacy & Compliance
Acme Kids Apps

Proposed attestation (for inclusion in your vendor file): "Redwood Inference hereby attests that, absent a separate written agreement specifying otherwise, it will not knowingly solicit, collect, or process personal data of children under the age of 13 on behalf of a customer. If a customer requires processing of data from children under 13, such processing will only occur where the customer provides documented verifiable parental consent and an approved processing annex is executed between the parties."

What we're sending you now (attached):
- redwood_coppa_attestation.txt (short attestation for your records)
- dpia_summary_2026-06.pdf (concise DPIA summary focused on minors' data processing risk and mitigations)
- redwood_dpa_sccs_v2.pdf (current DPA with SCCs; Lauren will route a DocuSign link for any redline)

- Evelyn can share example SDK config snippets for age-gating if Acme confirms whether you'll be sending birthdate or an age flag.
- If helpful, I can set a 30-minute call next week to walk the DPIA summary.

ETA: we aimed to meet your 6/11 request and have attached the items above. If Procurement needs specific SOC 2 artifacts or a completed SIG/CAIQ, Lauren can coordinate secure delivery (some items are shared under NDA).

Thanks — looking forward to getting this wrapped up.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

title: Operational constraints for log retention and residency — summary & next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Patel <aisha@clinitech.ai>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@clinitech.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-10T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up: operational constraints for logs/residency (post-demo recap)

Hi Aisha / Ravi —

Thanks again for today's demo and the very clear walk-through of Clinitech's data residency and PII handling constraints. Per our discussion, here's a compact recap of the operational constraints you outlined, plus proposed next steps from our side. Please correct any mischaracterizations.

Key constraints we captured:
- No persistent storage of raw user PII on shared hosted tiers; customer requires either zero-persistence for model inputs or a strict short-window retention (<= 48 hours) with KV redaction.
- Mandatory EU residency for EU patient data; US-only in region for US patient data with optional hybrid routing for analytics.
- Audit evidence for redaction/TTL enforcement required for SOC2 and procurement: logs showing purge events and per-request metadata for a rolling 30-day window.
- Encryption-at-rest + customer-managed KMS for Private deployments.

What Redwood can do (initial):
- Hosted API: demonstrate a 48-hour short-log window + prefix/KV redaction. We'll also validate our purge proof approach with a short sample report.
- Dedicated/Private: provide option to pin logs to customer VPC with customer KMS and configure TTLs down to 0 hours (no persistence) for qualified sessions.
- Routing: policy to route EU calls to eu-1 region and US to us-west by default; safe fallbacks if capacity limited.

Requested items from Clinitech to move forward:
1) A prioritized list of which endpoints need 0-retention vs which can accept short windows (48h / 7d).
2) A sanitized sample payload (PII redacted) or synthetic traffic shape for us to validate purge behavior and latency impact.
3) Any procurement or legal contacts for discussing a short-term pilot agreement and SOC2 evidence scope (you mentioned Legal would be involved).

Next steps (proposed timeline):
- By EOD 6/12: Redwood sends proposed pilot config (attachment: proposed_log_window_map_v1.xlsx) and a one-page purge-evidence template.
- Week of 6/15: Pilot kick-off (1 week) with sanitized traffic to validate 48h window and purge proofs.
- Post-pilot: decide on Dedicated vs Private and finalize KMS integration.

I've attached the proposed_log_window_map_v1.xlsx with mappings we discussed. Marissa / Jonas — can you take point on the pilot config doc and purge-evidence template?

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: proposed_log_window_map_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), Clinitech_PRIVACY_REQS.pdf (redlines from call)

From: Aisha Patel <aisha@clinitech.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@clinitech.ai>, Clinical Legal <legal@clinitech.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-10T09:05:00-07:00

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Patel <aisha@clinitech.ai>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@clinitech.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-10T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up: operational constraints for logs/residency (post-demo recap)

Hi Aisha / Ravi —

Thanks again for today's demo and the very clear walk-through of Clinitech's data residency and PII handling constraints. Per our discussion, here's a compact recap of the operational constraints you outlined, plus proposed next steps from our side. Please correct any mischaracterizations.

Key constraints we captured:
- No persistent storage of raw user PII on shared hosted tiers; customer requires either zero-persistence for model inputs or a strict short-window retention (<= 48 hours) with KV redaction.
- Mandatory EU residency for EU patient data; US-only in region for US patient data with optional hybrid routing for analytics.
- Audit evidence for redaction/TTL enforcement required for SOC2 and procurement: logs showing purge events and per-request metadata for a rolling 30-day window.
- Encryption-at-rest + customer-managed KMS for Private deployments.

What Redwood can do (initial):
- Hosted API: demonstrate a 48-hour short-log window + prefix/KV redaction. We'll also validate our purge proof approach with a short sample report.
- Dedicated/Private: provide option to pin logs to customer VPC with customer KMS and configure TTLs down to 0 hours (no persistence) for qualified sessions.
- Routing: policy to route EU calls to eu-1 region and US to us-west by default; safe fallbacks if capacity limited.

Requested items from Clinitech to move forward:
1) A prioritized list of which endpoints need 0-retention vs which can accept short windows (48h / 7d).
2) A sanitized sample payload (PII redacted) or synthetic traffic shape for us to validate purge behavior and latency impact.
3) Any procurement or legal contacts for discussing a short-term pilot agreement and SOC2 evidence scope (you mentioned Legal would be involved).

Next steps (proposed timeline):
- By EOD 6/12: Redwood sends proposed pilot config (attachment: proposed_log_window_map_v1.xlsx) and a one-page purge-evidence template.
- Week of 6/15: Pilot kick-off (1 week) with sanitized traffic to validate 48h window and purge proofs.
- Post-pilot: decide on Dedicated vs Private and finalize KMS integration.

I've attached the proposed_log_window_map_v1.xlsx with mappings we discussed. Marissa / Jonas — can you take point on the pilot config doc and purge-evidence template?

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: proposed_log_window_map_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), Clinitech_PRIVACY_REQS.pdf (redlines from call)

title: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, HealthLane Security <security@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Hi Jane / HealthLane team,

Thanks again for the conversation this week. Per our roadmap and the Order Form timeline, I wanted to kick off a short working thread to align on three things we need to close before legal redlines: (1) pen-test scope & timing, (2) SOC 2 / attestation delivery cadence, and (3) how evidence will be routed (ticketing + artifact naming).

From Redwood's side:
- We can accept a staging-only penetration test (no production access) and provide ephemeral creds for the window.
- SOC 2: we can provide the latest report plus a scoped list of control mappings; we normally deliver the full report on an annual cadence and can share an interim attestation in some cases.
- Evidence ticketing: we use a single JIRA board for evidence uploads (one ticket per artifact) and can create a templated ticket that maps to your evidence requests.

Attached: security_exhibit_v1.docx (draft exhibit with initial suggested language).

Asks: 
1) Do you have preferred pen-test windows (weeks in June/July)?
2) Is quarterly or annual SOC 2 delivery sufficient for procurement, or do you need something in between during onboarding?
3) Who should be the single point of contact on your side for evidence review?

If it helps we can set a 30-minute call next week to walk through the draft exhibit line-by-line.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
Ava.chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: security_exhibit_v1.docx (docx)]

From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthLane Security <security@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Hi Ava — thanks for the clear summary.

Quick answers: 
- Pen-test windows: we prefer mid-July (week of July 12) or the first two weeks of August if you need more runway. Staging-only is fine — we'll coordinate scoping details.
- SOC 2 cadence: Procurement prefers annual, but Security will ask for a short attestation during onboarding if there are any gaps; quarterly would be ideal, but not strictly required. For now, annual + onboarding attestation should cover internal approvals.
- POC: I'm the technical POC (Jane Doe) and procurement will be copied for contract steps.

Attaching our SOC 2 assertions spreadsheet to show the mapping we'll want verified.

Can your legal/controls team confirm whether you will accept a 30-day notice period for inspections and whether Redwood requires reciprocal rights? Procurement will need that for the Order Form.

Thanks,
Jane
Director, Security Operations — HealthLane
jane.doe@healthlane.com
[Attachment: HealthLane-soc2-assertions.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 08:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane — appreciate the quick reply and the assertions sheet. A few operational clarifications from engineering/security: 

- Pen-test scope: we'll scope to non-production endpoints only. We'll provide a staging environment with time-limited keys and an agreed test plan. Staging window preference: week of July 12 is workable for us.

- Inspection/notice: operationally, 30 days notice for an inspection or evidence pull is reasonable. For the MSA clause we typically avoid open-ended inspection rights (we ask for bounded windows and purpose-limited access). Attached is a short snippet with suggested pen-test + inspection language you can share with Procurement/legal.

- Evidence routing: recommend we create a single JIRA project (SEC-EVIDENCE-HealthLane) where each artifact (SOC 2 report, control screenshots, configuration exports) is uploaded to a ticket and linked back to your assertions spreadsheet. This keeps evidence immutable and auditable.

Next steps: I'll draft the pen-test clause and the JIRA template today and loop in Neha for legal redlines.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Security Operations — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: proposed_pen_test_clause_snippet.txt (txt)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane / team — looping in legal for a couple of redline points after reviewing Ben's snippet and Ava's draft exhibit. High level: we support staging-only pentest and the proposed ticketing model, but two contract items need explicit confirmation or alternate language from HealthLane procurement: 

1) Duration of inspection rights: the draft currently reads "inspection rights for the lifetime of the Agreement." We propose a bounded period tied to the Order Form term + 12 months post-termination for evidence access (to support certain compliance obligations). Please confirm if Procurement can accept that.

2) Notice for penetration testing / inspection: we propose a 30-day notice for scheduled inspections and a 72-hour emergency notification for incident-related forensic access (not for regular pentest windows).

If those two items are acceptable, we will incorporate them into the MSA redline. Also: please confirm whether HealthLane requires reciprocal audit rights into your systems — our standard position is that vendor inspection is one-way (HealthLane inspects Redwood), unless a separate forensic agreement is established.

I've attached the redline note PDF with inline comments for the exhibit. Neha will shepherd the legal changes through to the Order Form once we have confirmation on the two points above.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: msa_redline_comments.pdf (pdf)]

From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 10:52:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Laura/Ben/Ava — thanks for the detail and the proposed language. Procurement is comfortable with: 
- Bounded inspection window (Order Form term + 12 months post-termination)
- 30-day scheduled notice for planned inspections; 72-hour notification for incident triage only

Reciprocal rights: Procurement will not grant Redwood inspection rights into HealthLane systems. If forensic access is needed, we will coordinate on a case-by-case basis under an NDA and a scoped forensic agreement.

SOC 2 cadence: Procurement confirms annual delivery is sufficient; Security will request a short onboarding attestation (we can accept emailed attestation with linked JIRA tickets for artifacts during onboarding). HealthLane is open to bi-weekly evidence uploads during the first 6 weeks of integration, then monthly for the next quarter, then annual for steady-state. Can Redwood support that ramp?

We can confirm the July 12 window for the staging pentest. Procurement expects the Order Form signature in the next two weeks if legal redlines are incorporated.

Thanks — Jane
[Quoted text follows]

Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Great — appreciate the quick confirm, Jane. Summary & next steps from Redwood: 

ACTION ITEMS:
- Ben: finalize the pen-test + inspection clause and post to the shared drive (due EOD Wed).
- Neha: incorporate Laura's redline suggestions into the MSA + Order Form (target: draft redline for Procurement review by Fri).
- Ava: create the SEC-EVIDENCE JIRA project and the ticket template; invite HealthLane security + procurement to the board (I will send invites today).
- Ops: we'll schedule the staging pentest for the week of July 12; Ben to share a test plan and staging access checklist two weeks prior.

Links: 
- Working doc: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-security-exhibit-HL
- JIRA board: https://jira.redwood.com/browse/SEC-EVIDENCE-123
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/HL-2026-043

If there are no surprises from Procurement on the signed Order Form timeline, we'll target an onboarding kickoff the week after signature and follow the ramped evidence cadence Jane outlined (bi-weekly for 6 weeks, then monthly for the quarter, then annual steady-state).

Thanks everyone — I'll set the 30-minute redline walkthrough for Thu if that works for Procurement/legal.

Best,
Ava Chen
Sr. Customer Success Manager — Redwood Inference

title: Veridian: Ephemeral retention ask + commercial impact
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Rao <asha@veridian.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2026 14:12:00 +0000
Subject: Veridian: Ephemeral retention ask + commercial impact

Thanks again for taking the call today — quick recap and proposed next steps.

What we heard from Veridian:
- Primary ask: avoid persistent storage of user-identifiable PII in logs (they requested effectively 0-retention for PII fields).
- Secondary items: prefer short retention for non-PII operational logs (they suggested 48–72 hours), and options for EU residency for exported artifacts.
- Decision process: security and legal review internally; pilot approval needed from Head of Security (Asha) and procurement signoff (Tom).

Our commercial note / constraint:
- 0-retention for PII across all request/response KV cache paths requires either an internal engineering guardrail and custom audit approach or an elevated contract (pilot + eng scope). That impacts our implementation timeline (~3–6 weeks) and has a modest one-time professional services estimate.

Proposed compromise for pilot:
1) Scoped 0-retention for PII fields only — Redwood will: redact configured PII fields server-side before logs are written; retain operation-level telemetry (non-identifying) for 72 hours for troubleshooting.
2) Deliver an \"audit evidence matrix\" showing where data flows and what we can produce for SOC/audit requirements during the pilot.
3) Timebox: 90-day pilot with a roll-forward path to standard prod SLA or a negotiated MSA amendment.
4) Commercial: propose a $10k one-time pilot engineering fee for the custom redaction + evidence artifacts; waived if Veridian converts to a 12‑month Dedicated commitment.

Attachments: I attached a draft MA (veridian_ma_draft_v2.pdf) and a preliminary audit_evidence_matrix.xlsx that maps controls to output types.

Next steps (please confirm):
- Asha: can you confirm whether redaction+72h non-PII telemetry is workable for your legal/security reviewers?
- Tom: if security is OK, can procurement sign a pilot SOW or should we route to your procurement vendor terms first?
- Internal: Marissa/Karthik, can you review the attached matrix and estimate the PS effort so we can firm up the number?

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment stubs: [veridian_ma_draft_v2.pdf (37 KB)], [audit_evidence_matrix.xlsx (18 KB)]

From: Asha Rao <asha@veridian.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Wu <tom.wu@veridian.health>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2026 16:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Veridian: Ephemeral retention ask + commercial impact

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear summary. Quick clarifications from security side: 
1) By \"PII fields only\" do you mean field-level redaction at ingestion (server-side) with no ability to rehydrate the original within Redwood? We need an assurance that the raw PII is not stored in any long-term backup or analytics store.
2) For the 72-hour telemetry window: can we define what \"non-identifying\" means (e.g., request size, error codes, trace ids) and confirm that full transcripts containing PHI/PII will never be included in those telemetry artifacts?

Procurement note for Tom: we'll need pilot SOW language that includes an explicit data-handling annex (redaction description, retention window, controls, and breach notification timeline).

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>

Date: Tue, 08 Jun 2026 11:22:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Veridian: Ephemeral retention ask + commercial impact

Ben / Asha — looping in Karthik for the technical corner cases and sending a suggested implementation approach plus PS estimate.

Technical approach (high level):
- Ingestion redaction hook: configure blacklist/whitelist at the request parsing layer. PII fields flagged by Veridian will be redacted server-side before any logging sinks receive them. Redaction is irreversible at app level (we do not persist original payloads after redaction).
- Backups/analytics: we will ensure that redacted payloads are the only form that leaves the runtime. Nightly backups will only include redacted artifacts; we can provide a backup verification hash for audit.
- Telemetry window: we recommend 72 hours for operational logs (error rates, token counts, trace ids) and zero retention for any trace/span payloads that might contain residual text. We'll include a definition doc for \"non-identifying telemetry\" in the audit matrix.

PS estimate and timeline:
- Discovery & field mapping (1 week)
- Implement redaction hook + test harness (2 weeks)
- Backup verification + audit artifact generation (1 week)
- Pilot support and triage (4 weeks)
Total: ~8 weeks calendar (can overlap tasks to 6 weeks if prioritized)
One-time PS estimate: $12,000 (engineering + verification + evidence artifacts). If Veridian signs a 12-month Dedicated commitment within 30 days of pilot start, we propose crediting $10,000 of that fee back to them.

I've attached an updated audit_evidence_matrix.xlsx with the proposed definitions. Ben: feel free to share this matrix with Asha and Tom.

Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference

title: DevGarden developer onboarding & endpoint migration playbook
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira@devgarden.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-05T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Hi Ava / Monica,

Thanks again for the intro. Attached is our initial requirements doc (seed_integration_requirements.pdf) — it highlights our primary use-cases: chat assistants for product pages and server-side embedding indexing.

Quick list of things we want to confirm before the sandbox cutover:
- Recommended SDK initializers for backend (Python) and for our Node lambda workers
- Endpoint migration plan: sandbox -> staging -> prod (min downtime expectation)
- Retry/timeouts strategy for short-lived lambdas (15s max runtime)
- Rate limit handling: how to surface 429 vs transient throttles and how to resume safely
- Any telemetry hooks we should add to detect quality regressions after model pinning

Can we get a proposed mutual action plan and a short code snippet to unblock our engineers? We have a kickoff on Monday and would like a short pre-read.

Thanks,
Samira
Developer Relations
DevGarden Inc.

Attachment: seed_integration_requirements.pdf (PDF, 72KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@devgarden.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-05T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Hi Samira — great to meet you (virtually). Short answers and a playbook sketch below; attaching a one-page quickstart (redwood_sdk_quickstart.md) that your engs can use right away.

High level plan (3 sprints, estimate):
1) Sprint 0 (3 days): Sandbox SDK install + basic smoke tests against the sandbox inference endpoint.
2) Sprint 1 (1 week): Endpoint migration testing (sandbox -> staging), retries & telemetry instrumented, run load tests.
3) Sprint 2 (1 week): Production cutover + monitor and rollback guardrails.

Sandbox endpoints and migration notes:
- Sandbox: api.sandbox.redwood.ai (no billing; limited throughput).
- Staging: api.staging.redwood.ai (closer to prod perf).
- Prod: api.redwood.ai (final routing; pin model versions).
Migration approach: always validate on staging with the same model pin and request shape used in prod. Use canary rollout (5% traffic) for first 24–48h then increase.

Retries/timeouts guidance for short-lived lambdas:
- Client-side timeout: set request timeout to 10s for single-request lambdas (reserve 5s for function processing).
- Retries: use idempotent retry logic for safe calls (embeddings, reranks). For potentially non-idempotent generation calls, prefer server-side fallbacks.
- Backoff: 3 retries with exponential backoff (base=250ms, factor=2, jitter=0.25). Don't exceed overall lambda budget.

Rate-limiting best practices:
- Surface 429 separately from 5xx. 429 implies client throttling; implement token-bucket locally and pause non-critical jobs.
- Use windowed counters and request batching for embeddings to reduce unit cost.
- If you receive 429s during a rollout, automatically shift to a lower-capacity compatible model variant (we can map variants).

Quickcode: Python SDK init + call (minimal)

from redwood import RedwoodClient
client = RedwoodClient(api_key="REPLACE_ME", endpoint="https://api.sandbox.redwood.ai")
resp = client.generate(model="redwood-2-small", prompt="Summarize the product page in two bullets", timeout=10)
print(resp.text)

Node example (fetch + timeout)

const { RedwoodClient } = require('@redwoodai/sdk')
const client = new RedwoodClient({ apiKey: process.env.RW_KEY, endpoint: 'https://api.sandbox.redwood.ai' })
const resp = await client.generate({ model: 'redwood-2-small', prompt: 'Quick TL;DR', timeout: 10000 })
console.log(resp.text)

Telemetry hooks to add:
- Request id (x-rw-request-id) propagation
- Latency and token counts per request
- Quality checks: sample outputs stored for periodic evals (we can provide eval harness)

Next steps: I attached the quickstart and a draft mutual action plan (MAP-DevGarden-v1.xlsx) — Monica, can you take the MAP and create the initial milestones? Vivek, assigning you to confirm recommended backoff knobs on the runtime.

Best,
Ava Chen
Customer Success, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_sdk_quickstart.md (Markdown, 6KB)

From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: DevGarden Engineering <eng@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Thanks Ava — couple follow-ups from the eng team:
- How does Redwood's KV cache interact when we do streaming responses? Is the KV cache safe to reuse across short-lived lambda containers?
- For embedding batching: what's the recommended max batch size and how do we handle partial failures?

Forwarding this to your runtime SME — Vivek, could you advise?

—Tom

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

(see above)


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-06T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Quick runtime answers for Tom and Samira:
- KV cache & streaming: KV caches are tied to a serving instance; short-lived lambdas should treat KV as ephemeral. For stable caching across requests, use our prefix caching API (client-side) which stores validated prefixes in a small Redis-backed cache we manage for dedicated customers, or persist locally for repeated warm paths.

- Streaming + cache safety: Streaming doesn't change KV semantics, but avoid reusing partial stream state across distinct user sessions. Always initialize per-session state and rely on prefix caching only for identical prefix queries.

- Embedding batching guidance:
  * Recommended max batch size: 128 vectors per request (depends on vector dim; tune if large).
  * Partial failures: our API returns a per-item status for batch calls. Best practice: retry only failed items with a capped exponential backoff and merge results in-order to preserve indexing consistency.

- Concrete backoff knobs (example pseudo):
  retries = 3
  base_ms = 250
  for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
      delay = base_ms * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) * (1 + random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25))
      sleep(delay / 1000)

- Node/HTTP client settings: set socket timeout to 12s and a per-request header X-RW-Request-ID. For lambdas with 15s budget, we recommend synchronous calls only for embeddings and async streaming for long generations (or offload generation to a job queue).

I'll add these as comments in the MAP. Happy to pair with your engs for a live debug session.

—Vivek
Senior Runtime Engineer
Redwood Inference

Thanks Ava — couple follow-ups from the eng team:
- How does Redwood's KV cache interact when we do streaming responses? Is the KV cache safe to reuse across short-lived lambda containers?
- For embedding batching: what's the recommended max batch size and how do we handle partial failures?

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

(see above)


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-06T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Quick runtime answers for Tom and Samira:
- KV cache & streaming: KV caches are tied to a serving instance; short-lived lambdas should treat KV as ephemeral. For stable caching across requests, use our prefix caching API (client-side) which stores validated prefixes in a small Redis-backed cache we manage for dedicated customers, or persist locally for repeated warm paths.

- Streaming + cache safety: Streaming doesn't change KV semantics, but avoid reusing partial stream state across distinct user sessions. Always initialize per-session state and rely on prefix caching only for identical prefix queries.

- Embedding batching guidance:
  * Recommended max batch size: 128 vectors per request (depends on vector dim; tune if large).
  * Partial failures: our API returns a per-item status for batch calls. Best practice: retry only failed items with a capped exponential backoff and merge results in-order to preserve indexing consistency.

- Concrete backoff knobs (example pseudo):
  retries = 3
  base_ms = 250
  for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
      delay = base_ms * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) * (1 + random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25))
      sleep(delay / 1000)

- Node/HTTP client settings: set socket timeout to 12s and a per-request header X-RW-Request-ID. For lambdas with 15s budget, we recommend synchronous calls only for embeddings and async streaming for long generations (or offload generation to a job queue).

I'll add these as comments in the MAP. Happy to pair with your engs for a live debug session.

—Vivek
Senior Runtime Engineer
Redwood Inference

Hi Liam,

Either path will have trade-offs on timing and discount retention — I flagged both with finance internally.

Ben

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 3 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Prepay proposal — needs invoicing / tax path guidance (Novasoft)

Thanks Ben — summary below along with our recommendation and the trade-offs. I've pasted short action items at the bottom.

Options (high level):
A) Redwood Australia invoices in AUD (local entity)
   - Pros: fastest for AU procurement/PO, GST charged on invoice (10%) so Novasoft can claim input tax credits if registered, clean for accounting.
   - Cons: we need to register and file GST in Australia (compliance cost / quarterly returns); discount preservation depends — we recommend holding original 8% discount, but add a 100bp fee carve (effectively reducing our net discount) to cover registration and cashflow friction unless legal requires no change.

B) Redwood Singapore invoices in SGD / USD (foreign supplier) with Novasoft self-assessing GST (reverse charge)
   - Pros: avoids Redwood establishing AU legal presence; simpler for Redwood treasury and repatriation.
   - Cons: procurement often resists because their AP systems prefer local entity invoices; Novasoft tax team must be comfortable self-assessing GST; timing could delay PO by 2–3 weeks.

C) Redwood US invoices in USD (legacy path)
   - Pros: simplest for us operationally if we have existing US billing relationship.
   - Cons: Novasoft will face currency conversion and potential GST/self-assessment issues; may force them to centralize via India with WHT exposure.

WHT considerations (India):
- If Novasoft pays from an Indian entity (INR), local tax law may require withholding on cross-border service payments. The rate varies by service and treaty position — it can be materially different if a certificate of withholding exemption / DTAA reduced rate is available. We should ask Novasoft to confirm the paying entity and whether they can provide a tax residency certificate for Redwood to rely on.

FX / currency recommendation:
- Offer the customer two invoice choices: (1) AUD fixed for first 12 months at a pre-agreed AUD/USD rate (we absorb +/- 2% variability), or (2) USD invoicing with a published quarterly FX pass-through and reconciliations. For procurement simplicity, option (1) is usually preferred.

Commercial tradeoffs / discount impact:
- If we invoice from AU (Option A) and take on GST filings, we should preserve the headline 8% term discount but add a 1% implementation/ops uplift to cover compliance overhead.
- If Novasoft insists on SG/US invoices (Option B/C), keep full 8% discount but add language for gross-up if WHT is applied at payment.

Recommendation (what to present to Novasoft):
1) Produce two parallel quotes attached to the term sheet: (a) AUD invoice from Redwood AU (includes 10% GST; net-of-GST pricing shown) with preserved 8% term discount but 100bp ops uplift; (b) SGD/USD invoice from Redwood SG/US with net pricing and a WHT gross-up clause.
2) Provide a short tax checklist for Novasoft to accelerate their tax team's clearance (I've drafted one, see Tax-Checklist-NOVA-AU.docx).
3) Offer an AUD price-lock for 12 months (we'll absorb +/-2% FX band for year 1).

Action items:
- Ben: prepare the two quotes and attach tax checklist.
- Rishi: confirm whether we can technically split invoicing across entities (e.g., some components billed from AU, others from SG) and whether that impacts revenue recognition.
- I'll confirm the GST registration timing and estimated compliance costs.

Evelyn
Director, Finance | Redwood Inference
Evelyn.Hart@redwood.ai | +1 415 555 0199

title: Trial runway extension request — engineering blocker (AuroraTech)
preview:
From: Elena Morales <elena@auroratech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 28 May 2026 10:02:34 -0700
Subject: Re: POC next steps / SSO adapter failure

Hi Ben / Connor —

Quick update from our side: the team ran into a hard blocker while wiring up your SSO adapter against our identity provider. The integration fails intermittently during token exchange and we hit rate limits that we didn't see in staging. Our eng lead flagged a deeper protocol mismatch that will take at least 10 business days to stabilize.

We're still committed to the POC and love the product so far, but with the timeline slip we're going to run out of sandbox run time and our evaluation window ends next week. Can Redwood provide a short extension to the trial window plus a buffer of evaluation credits while we fix this? If we can't continue testing we'll lose the exec demo slot on June 15.

I've attached the failing request dump and a short error log. Happy to hop on a quick call with your engineers — we're free tomorrow afternoon PST.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Director of Platform
AuroraTech

Attachments: sso-error-log.txt (text)

To: Elena Morales <elena@auroratech.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>

title: Post-call recap: CreditLine discovery — token levers & budget constraints
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@creditline.ai>, Samir Patel <samir@paymentsops.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-06-04T14:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap: CreditLine discovery — token levers & budget constraints

Thanks again for taking time today — quick recap from our side and suggested next steps. I wanted to make sure we captured the pain, the target outcomes, and the minimum data we need to size an initial pilot.

Key pain points you confirmed
- High volume of short, templated customer messages (KYC status, limit updates) driving large token spend at scale.
- Latency variability during peak periods (end-of-month account runs) causing downstream retries.
- Lack of visibility into per-route token spend and no prefix caching for repeated prompts.

Business goals you called out
1) Reduce per-interaction cost by ~40–60% within a 90-day pilot (focus on templated flows).
2) Achieve stable p95 latency under 300ms for synchronous customer-facing flows.
3) Ability to forecast monthly token spend to fit a fixed budget envelope for Q3.

Technical levers we discussed (what we can turn on quickly)
- Batching windows for backend-to-backend flows (expected 20–35% token-cost reduction for async jobs).
- Prefix/KV caching for templated prompts (big win for repeated status checks).
- Quantized model variant for non-critical, high-throughput routes (trade: small quality delta for cost savings).
- Route-level fallback to smaller model when Dedicated capacity is constrained.

Initial sizing asks from Redwood to build a cost model
- Sample of 1 week of request logs (anonymized): headers, route name, avg prompt length, avg completion length.
- Expected concurrency profile (peak reqs/minute) and SLA target per route.
- Current spend baseline (monthly token counts by route if available).

Next steps
1) I’ll send a short deck + a first-pass token-econ spreadsheet by EOD (attached).
2) Elena / Samir: please share a redacted week of request logs and confirm which route(s) we should prioritize (templated-status, limit-increase, and dispute-autoresponder were candidates).
3) Schedule 30m ops call with your infra lead next week to confirm integration approach for KV caching and routing.

Attachments: creditline_postcall-deck.pdf (post-call summary), token-econ-1p-estimates.csv (first-pass estimates)

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference | (415) 555-0132



From: Elena Ruiz <elena@creditline.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@paymentsops.io>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-06-05T09:08:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: CreditLine discovery — token levers & budget constraints

Ben — thanks for the clear recap and the deck. A couple quick clarifications so we prioritize the right routes: 

1) Primary route for cost focus: templated-status (handles ~63% of user checks). Limit-increase is lower volume but high business value.
2) Logs: we can share a sanitized 7-day export with prompt/completion lengths and route tags. We’ll remove PII and account IDs. ETA for that is Friday EOD.
3) Latency: our internal SLA goal is p95 < 400ms for external-facing checks; the 300ms target you noted would be ideal but may require Dedicated capacity and tighter batching controls. Can you indicate what level of Dedicated commit would likely be required to reach 300ms?

Also, quick ask: can you flag what quality guardrails look like if we use a quantized variant for templated flows (we can tolerate very occasional truncation but not hallucination).

—Elena
Elena Ruiz
Head of Product, CreditLine
elena@creditline.ai

On Thu, Jun 4, 2026 at 2:12 PM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Thanks again for taking time today — quick recap from our side and suggested next steps. I wanted to make sure we captured the pain, the target outcomes, and the minimum data we need to size an initial pilot.
> 
> [original message trimmed]


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@creditline.ai>, Samir Patel <samir@paymentsops.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-06-05T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: CreditLine discovery — token levers & budget constraints

Elena — great, thanks. Two quick answers and I attached the spreadsheet with a first-pass Dedicated commit / cost trade table.

Dedicated guidance toward p95 latency targets
- To be conservative, reaching p95 ~300ms for synchronous external flows typically maps to a small Dedicated pool with modest commit (examples: 2 x A100-equivalent pods) depending on concurrency. The attached sheet includes 3 scenario rows: Minimal commit, Mid, and Aggressive.
- If your concurrency is spiky (e.g., end-of-month), we recommend burst capacity option so you don't overprovision baseline.

Quality guardrails for quantized variants
- Quantized model for templated-status: recommended for templated short outputs only. We advise A/Bing 10k requests before full roll-out. Expected delta: <1.5% accuracy change on templated slots, very low hallucination risk if prompts remain templated.

Attached: token-econ-1p-estimates.csv — includes route-level cost estimates, three Dedicated sizing scenarios, and estimated ROI vs current baseline. High level: with caching + batching and modest Dedicated commit we model 45–55% unit-cost reduction on templated-status routes.

Next steps (revised)
- CreditLine: deliver sanitized 7-day logs (Friday EOD).
- Redwood: run cost model + short ops plan; deliver a one-page pilot SOW draft by Monday.
- Ops call: I’ll propose Wed 6/10 10:00 PT; please confirm if that works for your infra lead.

Ben



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-06-06T09:30:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: next steps for CreditLine pilot

Looping in internal next steps after Ben’s thread — short checklist for our side: 
- Commercial: draft pilot SOW (1 page) and outline baseline metrics; include burst capacity clause.
- Solutions/Infra: prepare a quick runbook for KV cache integration paths (Redis vs Redwood-managed cache) and expected engineering effort (dev hours).
- Security: request basic SOC2 evidence packet once CreditLine confirms account and we sign an NDA.

Ben — please drop the proposed Wed 6/10 ops call time into my calendar and flag who from Infra we should invite.

Marissa
VP, Growth | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.inference

title: Final scoring: Devon Crawford — late votes and rubric clarifications
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Menon <priya@horizontalent.com>
Date: 2026-06-05T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: last-minute score submissions + rubric note for Devon Crawford

Hi all — quick reminder and a short clarification on rubric interpretation for Devon (onsite yesterday). We have three outstanding scorecards (I think Marcus has submitted but I didn't see Laura/Aditya final numbers). Hiring committee policy allows a single late submission by EOD where it materially changes the median.

A couple of clarifications based on the panel calibration doc: the "design tradeoffs" axis is meant to capture breadth of tradeoff discussion & evidence (0–4 scale). Some reviewers have been writing narrative notes instead of anchoring to 0/1/2/3/4; please pick the closest number and add a 1-sentence anchor (e.g., "chose performance over extensibility; gave tradeoff for X").

I've attached the scorecard template and started an aggregate CSV (panel_aggregate_20260605.csv) with what's in so far. If you can't get to a final numeric by 5pm PT, please reply with the top-line recommendation (strong/no hire/consider) and any hard blockers.

Next steps: if scores produce a tie or strong disagreement (>2-point spread on a 0–4 axis across reviewers), I propose a 15-min sync at 4:30pm PT to reconcile. Calendly link below if you want to block now.

Attachments: Crawford_scorecard_template.pdf (fillable), panel_aggregate_20260605.csv (WIP)

Thanks — Camila
Camila Reyes

camila.reyes@redwood.com

I don't want systematic bias because of different examples used by interviewers. Would appreciate a signal on which axis should dominate if only one can (we generally bias toward 'execution' for senior hires).

title: Post-POC follow-up: brief metrics recap + reference permission
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Martinez <alyssa@nebula.health>
Cc: Tom Rivera <tom@nebula.health>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-POC follow-up: brief metrics recap + reference permission

Hi Alyssa,

Thanks again for running the 3-week POC with us — the team at Redwood really enjoyed working with you and the Nebula Health data science folks. I promised a quick recap of the headline results and a small ask: would Nebula be willing to provide a short quote or join a 15–20 minute reference call?

Headline POC outcomes (3-week test, real infra + 10k samples):
- Inference latency (p95): reduced from ~420ms to 120ms when using Redwood batching + KV prefix cache
- Cost per successful inference: ~0.015 USD/token effective (estimated 34% savings vs baseline)
- Throughput: sustained 180 requests/min with auto-batching configuration
- Accuracy: model A NDCG@10 improved by 2.7% vs your prior baseline on the holdout set

Attached is a one-page summary (nebula_poc_summary.pdf) with the charts Tom and I discussed.

Ask: if the results above look right, would you be open to either: 1) a 2–3 sentence quote we can use in a case study, or 2) a quick 15–20 minute call next week where someone from your product/DS team can speak to the outcomes? If helpful, we can send a short set of suggested quote lines for edit.

Available times (Pacific): Mon 6/8 10–11am, Tue 6/9 9–11am, Wed 6/10 2–4pm. If none work, give me a couple windows that do.

Thanks again — wanted to make this as low-friction as possible.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

--
Attachment: nebula_poc_summary.pdf (10KB stub)

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Rivera <tom@nebula.health>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-POC follow-up: brief metrics recap + reference permission

Ben —

Really appreciate the clean recap and the one-pager. The numbers look in line with what we observed on our end; Tom ran an independent check on the throughput curve yesterday and confirmed the spike once caching was enabled.

We're happy to help. Option 1 is easiest: I'll draft a 2–3 sentence blurb from a product perspective that highlights the latency and cost savings. Tom is also open to a short call if you want a technical voice.

I can share a quote by end of day Friday (6/6). For the call, Thurs 6/11 10–10:30am PT works for Tom and me.

Go ahead and send the suggested lines — I may tweak them but that should speed things up. Also confirm whether we need to sign any permission form to allow Redwood to publish the quote/company name.

Thanks,
Alyssa Martinez
Director of Product, Nebula Health
alyssa@nebula.health | +1 617-555-0147

--

- "Working with Redwood during the pilot let us scale inference throughput predictably without investing in additional GPU ops."

- Inference latency (p95): reduced from ~420ms to 120ms when using Redwood batching + KV prefix cache
- Cost per successful inference: ~0.015 USD/token effective (estimated 34% savings vs baseline)
- Throughput: sustained 180 requests/min with auto-batching configuration
- Accuracy: model A NDCG@10 improved by 2.7% vs your prior baseline on the holdout set

Attached is a one-page summary (nebula_poc_summary.pdf) with the charts Tom and I discussed.

Ask: if the results above look right, would you be open to either: 1) a 2–3 sentence quote we can use in a case study, or 2) a quick 15–20 minute call next week where someone from your product/DS team can speak to the outcomes? If helpful, we can send a short set of suggested quote lines for edit.

title: Forecast definitions refresh and quick training: commit / best-case / pipeline tags
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Finance Ops <finance@nimbus.co>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Forecast definitions refresh and quick training: commit / best-case / pipeline tags

Morning team,

Finance flagged that our month-end commit accuracy has drifted and asked for a tighter, shared set of definitions for: Commit, Best Case, and Pipeline. This should reduce subjective toggling and make our forecast pulls more actionable. I drafted a short set of proposed definitions (attached) and would like us to align quickly so we can: update stage mappings in HubSpot, roll the checklist out to AEs, and start a short commit-review cadence.

Summary (proposal):
- Commit: Deal with an active signed PO/contract pending legal or final procurement approval OR a verbal agreement + dedicated internal budget owner; sales-recommended close date within the quarter and champion committed to execute procurement steps within 30 days.
- Best Case: High confidence (>=60%) based on product-fit evidence and stakeholder alignment, but with one outstanding dependency (e.g., procurement timeline, SOW redlines, or executive approval).
- Pipeline (all other open opportunities): Opportunities with interest and ongoing conversation but lacking the alignment or budget evidence to be Best Case or Commit.

Quick asks:
1) Review the attached "forecast-definitions-v2.pdf" and suggest edits by EOD Thu.
2) Confirm who is owning mapping to HubSpot Stage / Probability buckets (I can help).
3) Pick a training slot: I added two 20-min options — Fri 9am PT or Mon 11am PT. Please accept the calendar invite.

Example to think about: Acme Corp (RDW-40123) — Karthik, see below, your context will help finalize the Commit criteria.

Attachments: forecast-definitions-v2.pdf (summary + examples)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

---
