title: AuthZ collection request — Four-eyes audit for Veridian Health
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Pamela Nguyen <pamela.nguyen@veridianhealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: AuthZ collection request — Four-eyes audit for Veridian Health

Hi Pamela,

Thanks for the quick sync earlier. Per Veridian's SOC 2 scope we want to assemble a focused access-control packet that your audit team can review for the upcoming "four-eyes" entitlement review. To keep this scoped and reviewable, can you confirm the specific artifacts you prefer first? Common asks are:

- RBAC snapshot for production org (role -> members) as of 2026-06-01
- SCIM delta covering provisioning events for the prior 90 days (CSV/JSONL)
- SAML assertion/examples for SSO flows (sample timestamps + attributes)
- Owners mapping (role owners, approvers) and any high-risk service accounts

We can deliver via encrypted Drive folder (shared link) or SFTP; happy to deliver both. If you can confirm preferred format and whether the team needs signed hashes for chain-of-custody, we will package and deliver by EOD Wednesday (06/03).

Looping Jordan (on-product) and Soojin (security engineering) to coordinate extraction and redaction.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: none in this message


From: Pamela Nguyen <pamela.nguyen@veridianhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Veridian Health Security <security@veridianhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 11:03:24 -0700
Subject: Re: AuthZ collection request — Four-eyes audit for Veridian Health

Ben —

Thanks, this looks good. For the initial review we need the following minimal set in this order of priority:

1) RBAC snapshot (CSV) with role id, role name, member ids (email), assignment timestamp, and a flag for owner/steward.
2) SCIM delta events covering 2026-03-01 to 2026-05-31 (we will accept JSONL).
3) Two SAML assertion samples that include the attributes you actually send (nameid, groups, email, iss, audience, inResponseTo). Redact PII except for the attributes themselves.
4) Owners map with email and management chain for each privileged role.

Two policy clarifications: we will not accept production log extracts without an integrity hash (SHA256) and a short explanation of any redaction. Also please mark any service accounts that can escalate to super-admins.

Preferred delivery: Google Drive link + HubSpot ticket ID. Please attach the HubSpot ticket number in your reply so our intake automation can file and triage.

Can you deliver the first bundle by EOD Wednesday (06/03)? If not, indicate the ETA.

Regards,
Pamela Nguyen
Director, Security Assurance — Veridian Health
security@veridianhealth.com

On Mon, Jun 1, 2026 at 09:15 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Pamela,
> 
> Thanks for the quick sync earlier...


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 08:21:10 -0700
Subject: Fwd: AuthZ collection request — Four-eyes audit for Veridian Health

Jordan / Soojin —

Forwarding Pamela's prioritized list. Can you pull the following and place in the shared folder:

- rbac_snapshot.csv (role_id, role_name, member_email, assigned_at, owner_flag)
- scim_delta_20260601.jsonl (filter events 2026-03-01 -> 2026-05-31)
- saml_assertion_samples.txt (two sanitized assertions; include the original XML and the redaction note)

title: Next steps: tech kickoff & auth choices for Coraline Health POC
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Zoe Martin <zoe.martin@coraline.health>, Alex Chen <alex.chen@coraline.health>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap — Integration scope & auth options

Hi Zoe / Alex —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our discovery so everyone is aligned and we can move to a technical kickoff: 

What we covered:
- Target flows: patient-facing summarization + internal search over clinical notes (embedding + reranker).
- Traffic profile: ~150 QPS peak after go-live, tail latency target ~150ms per token for the summarization route.
- Data residency: US-only; requires audit trails and key rotation policy.

Integration considerations we flagged (items for the tech kickoff):
1) SDK + routing: whether you use Redwood SDK or direct REST calls. We recommend SDK for streaming + auto-reconnect.
2) Auth: three feasible options — short-lived service accounts (JWT), API keys with rotation, or OAuth2 for user-scoped requests.
3) Key management: you mentioned wanting HSM/KMS integrations and automated key rotation.
4) Structured output/tool calling: desire to return JSON with strict schema for downstream ingestion.
5) Embeddings vs rerankers: embed index in your VPC and use lightweight reranker on Redwood for highest recall.

Attached a brief integration checklist and an auth-options matrix so you can review.

Next steps (proposed):
- Schedule 60m tech kickoff (solutions engineer + infra) — please suggest times next week.
- Decide an initial auth pattern: we can pilot with API keys and a rotation policy, or set up a JWT service account flow if you prefer no long-lived keys.
- Marcus to draft a minimal routing config + sample policy for caching/kv prefixes.

If you want, Marcus and Neha can join a 30m pre-kickoff to draft the config before the broader call.

Thanks,
Camila
Account Exec | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: redwood-integration-checklist.pdf, auth-options-matrix.xlsx


From: Zoe Martin <zoe.martin@coraline.health>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Chen <alex.chen@coraline.health>
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 17:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap — Integration scope & auth options

Camila — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifying questions from our platform/security team: 

1) For the JWT/service account option, will Redwood issue the private key or do we bring our own (BYOK)? We need to know because our compliance team prefers keys in our KMS.
2) Key rotation cadence: do you support automatic rotation with zero-downtime for active sessions?
3) Structured output — can we enforce a strict JSON schema server-side so that malformed responses fail the request rather than produce best-effort text?

Alex and I are available Thurs or Friday morning PT for a short tech sync. We also flagged wanting audit logs for token issuance and rotation events.

—Zoe
Zoe Martin, VP Product
Coraline Health
zoe.martin@coraline.health


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Zoe Martin <zoe.martin@coraline.health>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Alex Chen <alex.chen@coraline.health>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap — Integration scope & auth options

Hi Zoe / Alex —

Couple quick answers while we set up the kickoff: 

- BYOK: Yes, we support customer-managed keys via KMS integrations (GCP KMS, AWS KMS, and HSM partners). For the JWT flow you can host the private key in your KMS and Redwood will accept signed assertions for service accounts.

- Rotation: Redwood supports staged key rotation: issue new key, update active tokens, and phase out old keys. For API keys we recommend short TTLs (24h refresh) for sessions and a rotating root key for service credentials. We can provide a rotation playbook that uses the SDK to fetch new credentials without interrupting streaming sessions.

- Strict schemas: yes — our structured output feature can be configured with a server-side validator (JSON Schema) and a strict mode that rejects outputs that don't match. That will return a 422 and a structured error you can parse.

I've attached the auth-options-matrix with pros/cons and sample timelines for rotation. Marcus — can you draft the minimal sample routing + example SDK snippet for their streaming summarization route?

Thanks,
Neha
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.ai


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Zoe Martin <zoe.martin@coraline.health>, Alex Chen <alex.chen@coraline.health>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 11:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap — Integration scope & auth options

Hi all —

Below is a minimal architecture suggestion and a sample SDK snippet for service-account JWT authentication and streaming summarization. This keeps keys in your KMS and uses short-lived tokens for the service to talk to Redwood.

Architecture (recommended for Coraline POC):
- Coraline KMS holds key material (BYOK).
- Internal service signs JWT assertions with KMS; Redwood validates assertion via your public key/thumbprint.
- Token TTL ~5m for streaming sessions; refresh logic in SDK.
- Embeddings index lives in your VPC; Redwood routes rerank requests over an encrypted peering link.
- Structured output: enable strict JSON Schema on summarization route.

Sample pseudocode (Node.js) — service account JWT flow + refill on refresh:

const jwt = signAssertionWithKMS({keyId: 'projects/..../keys/sa-key'});
const tokenResponse = await fetch('https://api.redwood.ai/v1/auth/token', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ assertion: jwt })
});
const { access_token, expires_in } = await tokenResponse.json();

// SDK use (streaming)
const client = new RedwoodSDK({ token: access_token });
const stream = client.summarize.stream({ input: clinicalNote, schema: 'coraline/summ-v1' });
stream.on('data', chunk => processChunk(chunk));

Notes on rotation: keep a small overlap window (e.g., 10s) when swapping keys. Our SDK can do a background refresh and swap active token handles atomically to avoid reconnects.

I'll put a short routing example into the Drive folder linked in the metadata. Proposed times for the kickoff: Thu 4 Jun 2026 16:00 PT or Fri 5 Jun 2026 09:00 PT. Which works?

–Marcus
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Zoe Martin <zoe.martin@coraline.health>, Alex Chen <alex.chen@coraline.health>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap — Integration scope & auth options

Zoe/Alex — great thread, thanks.

Quick summary and agreed next steps: 
- Auth approach for POC: start with JWT service account + BYOK in your KMS (per your compliance request). Marcus will drop the exact JWT exchange flow and validate steps in the Drive doc.
- Rotation: implement 5m streaming tokens with root key rotation per the attached playbook. Redwood will emit audit events for token issuance/rotation to your SIEM via our event webhook.
- Structured outputs: enable strict JSON Schema on summarization route (we'll include an initial schema you can iterate on).
- Kickoff: Thu 4 Jun 2026 16:00 PT (I'll send an invite for 60m).

Action owners:
- Marcus: share the Drive link with sample routing, SDK snippet, and token-exchange details (by EOD).
- Neha: share rotation playbook and example audit webhook payloads (by EOD).
- Camila (me): schedule kickoff + prep agenda and share HubSpot deal link.

If anything else comes up before the call, flag it here. Looking forward to getting this running for Coraline.

Best,
Camila
Account Exec | Redwood Inference

title: Marketplace renewal: acceptance timing & burst capacity synchronization
preview:
From: Elaine Chu <elaine@medstreamhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Josh Kramer <josh.kramer@cloudmarketplace.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal & planned capacity increase — syncing acceptance window

Hi Ben,

Quick note as we ramp into our July workload: MedStream is planning to increase committed GPU capacity by ~35% starting 2026-07-15 to support a new image-processing pipeline. - Acceptance window: We can target private-offer acceptance on the 2026-07-10 date you requested. AWS typically processes the private offer acceptance within 24–48 hours, but we should leave a 72-hour buffer for marketplace propagation.
- Burst capacity: We can stand up a temporary burst pool for 30 days post-acceptance. I’ll need to confirm exact GPU types and cost for the burst; we can do a short addendum that covers the 30-day burst at an agreed uplift rate.
- PO flow: Net-30 is fine. Karthik — can you confirm our invoicing cadence if the private offer is accepted on 7/10 and effective on 7/15? Also looping Marissa for legal on the addendum language and any acceptance sequencing that impacts the PO.

Next steps:

2) I’ll draft a short acceptance timeline and a 30-day burst addendum we can attach to the PO/DocuSign package.

Best,
Ben Carter

- For the 30-day burst: recommend we invoice the burst as a separate line item dated the acceptance effective date so there’s no mingling with committed capacity invoices. That makes reporting easier for both sides.

Elaine / team — legal ok on the approach; suggested flow below to avoid acceptance/contract sequencing issues with AWS marketplace:

2) MedStream issues PO_02459 referencing the private offer ID and the burst addendum. PO should state ‘acceptance subject to AWS Private Offer ID XXXXX’.
3) Redwood executes addendum and returns for signature.
4) MedStream accepts private offer in AWS and uploads signed addendum to the DocuSign link.

We recommend Redwood sign the addendum first to ensure the burst terms are locked — otherwise AWS may surface the private offer before both parties have executed addendum language and that can create invoice mismatch risk.

Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, accounts@redwoodinference.com
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor documents for Redwood PO setup

Hi Lisa — thanks for sending these over and for the quick turnaround. Two quick items I need to resolve with you before I can push this into procurement for PO creation:

1) Entity / TIN status: Our onboarding flow flagged the W-8BEN-E because the form is valid for non-US entities. However, when the bank letter was OCR'ed the tax id field looked like a US EIN (9-digit format) which triggers a mismatch in our validation. Could you confirm the tax identifier you want recorded in Redwood (EIN vs foreign TIN), and whether Nimbus is electing foreign withholding treaty benefits?

2) If Nimbus is actually US-taxable (has a US EIN) we require a signed W-9 instead of a W-8. If you're non-US (W-8 is correct), we still need: (a) a clear statement of country of tax residence on company letterhead, and (b) a matching tax identifier that aligns with the W-8.

For speed, I'm copying Ben from Procurement and attaching our short W-9 request template that legal asks vendors to complete when we need EIN confirmation (Redwood_W9_Request_Template.docx). If you can confirm which path applies, I'll route accordingly.

If you prefer a quick call, I have 10–11am PST today.

Thanks — Avery
Avery Johnson
Vendor Onboarding Lead | Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com

Senior Procurement Analyst | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com



From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@nimbus-analytics.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Jun 2026 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor documents for Redwood PO setup — quick legal check

Hi all — quick guidance from Legal: we can proceed with a limited, conditional PO in very narrow circumstances, provided we have three items on file before any payment is released:

1) A copy of the vendor-signed W-8BEN-E OR W-9 (if vendor confirms US EIN). If vendor cannot provide a W-9 immediately, the W-8 must be unequivocal and the tax id on the bank letter must be a clearly non‑US identifier (not just formatted like a US EIN).
2) A vendor letter on company letterhead confirming tax residence and signatory authority (dated within 30 days).
3) A notarized or bank-signed letter evidencing the tax identifier (your Nimbus_Bank_Letter.pdf may suffice if it includes bank letterhead and a bank contact).

If those three items are present we will allow Procurement to issue a short-duration PO labeled "conditional — pending tax verification". Final payment will be withheld until Legal confirms the record alignment. Please note: if Nimbus intends to be treated as US-taxable (W-9/EIN) and refuses to supply a W-9, Redwood must treat payments as subject to backup withholding risk.

Lisa — to speed this up I can prepare a one-click DocuSign packet (W-9 + short vendor attestation) that Nimbus can sign electronically. Sending link now: https://docusign.com/some-doc-id-xyz.

Let me know if you want me to issue the DocuSign packet to the accounts email.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com
Phone: +1 650-555-0123

title: Post-call recap: Aligning multi-zone availability for technical deep dive
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Erin Zhao <erin.zhao@healthsprout.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap: Aligning multi-zone availability for technical deep dive

Hi Erin — thanks again for a productive discovery call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can lock a technical deep dive with the right folks (product + infra):

Key takeaways from today's call:
- Primary use case: real-time inference for patient-facing triage (low-latency, audit logs).
- Expected throughput: bursty peaks ~150 rps; trailing average ~25 rps.
- Data constraints: PHI-filtered inputs; prefer VPC/private deployment and KMS integration.
- Success metrics: <150ms p50 inference, predictable monthly spend.

Immediate asks / next steps:
1) Schedule a 60–75 minute technical deep dive with your infra lead (Samir) + one product PM.
2) We'll demo a short, instrumented run on our staging model and walk through routing/rollout options.
3) Marissa will prepare a short checklist (compliance / data flow) and share sample config.

To make scheduling straightforward across your US/EU/APAC stakeholders I sketched a lightweight rotating-window approach (keeps the same weekday for continuity, rotates the anchor time so teams in each region get a reasonable local hour once every 3 weeks):

Proposed rotation pattern (example weeks):
- Week A: 10:00 PT / 18:00 CET / 02:00 SGT (good for US morning + EU evening)
- Week B: 14:00 PT / 22:00 CET / 06:00 SGT (shifts later for EU/early APAC)
- Week C: 08:00 PT / 16:00 CET / 00:00 SGT (APAC earlier slot)

If that framing looks ok, could you and Samir indicate which of these anchors works best next week? Alternatively you can pick any open slot on this Calendly and we'll book the deep dive: https://calendly.com/redwood/tech-deep-dive

I'll attach a short list of technical questions and a lightweight agenda so we can keep the session focused. Marissa — can you assemble the short checklist and attach?

Thanks — really looking forward to a deep dive.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142


From: Erin Zhao <erin.zhao@healthsprout.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@healthsprout.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 18:02:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: Aligning multi-zone availability for technical deep dive

Thanks Aditya — helpful recap. Quick notes from our side: Samir and I reviewed the pattern and like the rotation idea because it avoids repeatedly asking the same team to take late calls. A couple of items: 

- Samir (cc'd) is the infra lead and will want the stubbed config + expected resource footprint before the call.
- Our PM (Aisha Kim) will join; I can loop her once we confirm the time.
- Preferred anchors: Week A or Week C (Samir prefers not to do the late-night EU/early APAC slot).

We can do Week A next Tuesday (2026-06-09) at your Week A anchor if that works for you: 10:00 PT / 18:00 CET / 02:00 SGT. Alternatively, if Week A next week is full for you, Week C on 06/10 also works for a shorter 60-minute window.

I'll grab a slot on the Calendly now and share the invite.

— Erin
Erin Zhao
Head of Product | HealthSprout
erin.zhao@healthsprout.com

Subject: Post-call recap: Aligning multi-zone availability for technical deep dive

Hi Erin — thanks again for a productive discovery call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can lock a technical deep dive with the right folks (product + infra):

Key takeaways from today's call:
- Primary use case: real-time inference for patient-facing triage (low-latency, audit logs).
- Expected throughput: bursty peaks ~150 rps; trailing average ~25 rps.
- Data constraints: PHI-filtered inputs; prefer VPC/private deployment and KMS integration.
- Success metrics: <150ms p50 inference, predictable monthly spend.

Immediate asks / next steps:
1) Schedule a 60–75 minute technical deep dive with your infra lead (Samir) + one product PM.
2) We'll demo a short, instrumented run on our staging model and walk through routing/rollout options.
3) Marissa will prepare a short checklist (compliance / data flow) and share sample config.

To make scheduling straightforward across your US/EU/APAC stakeholders I sketched a lightweight rotating-window approach (keeps the same weekday for continuity, rotates the anchor time so teams in each region get a reasonable local hour once every 3 weeks):

Proposed rotation pattern (example weeks):
- Week A: 10:00 PT / 18:00 CET / 02:00 SGT (good for US morning + EU evening)
- Week B: 14:00 PT / 22:00 CET / 06:00 SGT (shifts later for EU/early APAC)
- Week C: 08:00 PT / 16:00 CET / 00:00 SGT (APAC earlier slot)

If that framing looks ok, could you and Samir indicate which of these anchors works best next week? Alternatively you can pick any open slot on this Calendly and we'll book the deep dive: https://calendly.com/redwood/tech-deep-dive

I'll attach a short list of technical questions and a lightweight agenda so we can keep the session focused. Marissa — can you assemble the short checklist and attach?

Thanks — really looking forward to a deep dive.

Best,
Aditya Rao

Hi Oliver —

This version is tailored toward a small clinical pilot and emphasizes a Week0 technical validation so your data/security team has a clear checklist.

What's included in the share:
- GDoc: Onramp Blueprint (editable) — proposed timeline, stakeholder lanes, acceptance criteria table (link below).

Ask: Can you confirm the primary owners on your side for "Data Access" and "Clinical Ops" so we can populate the RACI? Also flag any hard no-go dates in June/July.

Quick notes on flow:

- Week2: Expand to additional clinics + throughput checks.

title: Azure Marketplace publisher account + offer packaging checklist and next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya.menon@microsoft.com>, isv-onboarding@microsoft.com, Sarah Jones <sarah.jones@acme.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Azure Marketplace publisher account + offer packaging checklist and next steps

Hi Priya / ISV team / Sarah,

Thanks for the quick sync last week — following up with a concise list of the artifacts we need to move the offer through Publisher Center validation and the technical review. ACME is ready to proceed and I want to ensure we have no last-minute gaps.

High-level ask (we can hand off artifacts to the ISV onboarding inbox once ready):
- Publisher account: business verification (company legal name, tax ID), primary publisher contact, MPN ID
- Offer artifacts: marketplace manifest (JSON), ARM templates (for VM/container deployment), container image location + tag, SKU metadata CSV
- Security/compliance: SSA scan or third-party vulnerability report, minimal runtime permissions doc, data residency statement
- Testing: a short smoke-test script, sample tenant with demo credentials or detailed test steps

Do you have any specific size or packaging constraints for ARM templates or zipped images? Also let me know if we should submit the artifacts to the Partner Center directly or if you'd prefer us to upload to the isv-onboarding mailbox first.

Proposed next step: I can bundle a draft manifest and the ARM template early this week and loop in our infra owner (Hiro) for final sign-off.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

---

On Tue, 2 Jun 2026 at 09:18 Priya Menon <priya.menon@microsoft.com> wrote:
> From: isv-onboarding@microsoft.com on behalf of Priya Menon <priya.menon@microsoft.com>
> To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sarah Jones <sarah.jones@acme.com>
> Cc: isv-onboarding@microsoft.com
> Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 09:18:00 +00:00
> Subject: Re: Azure Marketplace publisher account + offer packaging checklist and next steps
>
> Hi Ben / Sarah,
>
> Thanks — this is exactly the level of detail we need. A couple of quick clarifications from the Partner Center side:
>
> 1) Publisher account verification: we need the company legal name and MPN (Microsoft Partner Network) ID before we can publish. If ACME does not have an MPN, please open a partner registration; we can fast-track verification once that's complete.
>
> 2) Offer package size: ARM templates should be under 5MB when zipped. If you have large container images, provide a registry path (ACR or Docker Hub) and tag — we do not require the image binary in the upload.
>
> 3) Technical validation: our standard checks are schema/manifest correctness, deployment smoke test, role-based access checks, and a basic security scan. The SSA scan you mentioned is perfect — please attach it.
>
> 4) Co-sell enablement: once published, you can request co-sell by filling the co-sell questionnaire in Partner Center (we'll send the link). For this account, please note any preferred co-sell regions (US/EU/APAC).
>
> If you can send the manifest + ARM artifacts to isv-onboarding@microsoft.com, we will kick off the automated validation and get back within 48 hours with any errors.
>
> Priya
> ISV Onboarding, Microsoft Azure
> priya.menon@microsoft.com
>
> --

> Ben — quick status:
>
> - Manifest: draft is ready (redwood-marketplace-manifest.json). It includes 2 SKUs (standard, enterprise) and a simple per-month subscription price and meter definitions. We added minimal metadata and a link to our docs.

title: Recap & next steps — Redwood demo / legal path
preview:
From: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Li <samantha.li@bluepeak.com>, Rajesh Patel <r.patel@bluepeak.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recap & next steps — Redwood demo / legal path

Hi Samantha / Rajesh —

Thanks again for carving out time today. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we stay aligned on the procurement path and legal routing: 

Summary of call
- Demo focused on inference latency/cost controls, Dedicated vs Hosted options, and private deployment requirements.
- BluePeak priority: predictable P95 latency, audit logging, and an on-prem option for PHI workloads.
- Initial volume estimate: ~400k tokens/day across test and pilot workloads.

Key stakeholders (please confirm or update)
- Economic buyer: Rajesh Patel (Procurement) — controls budget and timeline
- Technical lead: Samantha Li (Infra/ML) — validation + pilot success metrics
- Legal/Compliance: Elena Morales (Legal) — NDA/MSA/DPA review
- Security: (you indicated a SecOps contact; share when ready)

Proposed legal sequence & timing (our recommendation)
1) Mutual NDA — quick form to surface data classification questions (target 3 business days)
2) DPA (if PHI/ePHI applies) — discuss required clauses in parallel with NDA review
3) MSA + SOW — rolling redlines after NDA sign, target 7–10 business days for first pass

Immediate asks
- Please confirm who at BluePeak will be the final signer for the MSA and the contact info for Elena Morales so we can route the NDA correctly.
- We'll attach a short-form NDA for Elena to kickoff review. If you'd prefer to start with your template, please send it and we'll prioritize that.

Attachments: BluePeak_NDA_v1.docx (short-form, editable)

Next steps
- I'll follow up with a one-page mutual action plan and an internal owner map.
- If it helps, Ben can propose a redline sequencing cadence (owner -> turn times -> fallback).

Thanks — Tessa

-- 
Tessa Morgan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[t] +1 (415) 555-0123 | tessa.morgan@redwood.com
Redwood Inference


From: Rajesh Patel <r.patel@bluepeak.com>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Samantha Li <samantha.li@bluepeak.com>
Date: Sun, 31 May 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap & next steps — Redwood demo / legal path

Tessa — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from BluePeak's side: 

Internal owners for legal/procurement
- Legal review: Elena Morales (elena.morales@bluepeak.com) — please include her on NDA routing.
- Procurement & signature: I own the final commercial approval, but executive signature will be by our CFO (to be confirmed after procurement).
- Security questionnaire / vendor risk: I'll loop in our SecOps lead (Mike Chen) separately for the security questionnaire.

Timeline realities
- For NDAs we can typically turn in ~5 business days if routed directly to Elena.
- MSA review historically takes 2–3 weeks depending on redlines; our procurement prefers to see a short-form NDA first.

DPA / PHI notes
- We do plan to move PHI into the pilot environment for a subset of models. That will require our legal and security teams to sign off on a DPA and possibly a Data Processing Addendum that references SOC 2 and encryption controls.

Action: Elena is on CC; please send the NDA and any short redline guidance. We'll take a look and I'll get back with a rough timeline for each step by mid-week.

Thanks,
Rajesh

--
Rajesh Patel
Head of Procurement, BluePeak Health
rajesh.patel@bluepeak.com | +1 (617) 222-9987


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Samantha Li <samantha.li@bluepeak.com>, Rajesh Patel <r.patel@bluepeak.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap & next steps — Redwood demo / legal path

Adding internal legal/contract routing guidance so expectations are clear: 

Redwood recommended flow
1) NDA (mutual) — goal: 3 biz days internal review, up to 5 biz days with customer redlines. Use short-form to avoid MSA drift.
2) DPA review in parallel — Rishi (cc'd) will own the DPA technical appendix and mapping to our SOC2 controls.
3) MSA + SOW — once NDA/DPA aligned, route MSA to Contracts queue (contracts@redwood.com) with a proposed SOW for pilot. Aim for first-pass comments within 7 business days.

Turn-times & owner table (high level)
- NDA: Redwood (Tessa / Contracts) -> BluePeak (Elena) — 3–5 biz days
- DPA: Redwood (Rishi) -> BluePeak (Legal/SecOps) — 7–10 biz days for first draft comments
- MSA: Redwood Contracts -> BluePeak Procurement/Legal -> Exec Review — 7–15 biz days depending on redlines

Attachments included: redline-guidance.pdf (a suggested ordering for clause-by-clause handling) and turn-times-table.xlsx

If BluePeak prefers a reverse-queue (i.e., they provide their template first), we can pivot and prioritize their redlines. Otherwise we recommend NDA-first to keep pilots moving.

Ben
-- 
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0167

title: Renewal: LumenAI scale plan & temporary price-hold request
preview:
From: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@lumen.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@lumen.ai>
Date: Thu, 14 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Ben / Monica,

Thanks for the partnership — LumenAI is coming up on our annual renewal and usage is trending ~3.4x this year vs. last. We have a hard internal window where predictable unit economics are required to commit headcount for our new product line.

Ask: would Redwood entertain a temporary 12-month price-hold at our current blended rate if we increase our committed capacity from 2.5M tokens/month to 6M tokens/month (tiered ramp over 3 months)? We’d also like a small bucket of credits (one-time) to offset onboarding for the new team, and a short-term burst guarantee for holiday spikes.

Attached an updated SOW and current usage report. Procurement is looped for contract language. Happy to jump on a quick call this week to align next steps.

Appreciate it,
Alex
Head of Platform, LumenAI
Attachments: LumenAI_SOW_v2.pdf (application/pdf), LumenAI-usage-report-May2026.csv (text/csv)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@lumen.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Alex — thanks for the context and the SOW. Congrats on the growth — exciting product roadmap.

Quick read: we can structure a commercial that gets you the predictability you need while protecting both sides on utilization. High level proposal:
- Ramp commit to 6M tokens/month over 90 days (2.5M -> 4M -> 5M -> 6M).
- Temporary price-hold: 9 months at current blended rate, with a review at month 9 to extend to 12 based on adoption signals (usage >85% of committed).
- One-time onboarding credit: $40k applied as invoice credit, spread over first 3 months.
- Burst insurance: allow 25% over-commit burst at a pre-agreed overage rate (lower than on-demand) for up to 30 days per year.

We’ll need to confirm a few items before locking: SOC 2 evidence for procurement, data residency clause you may require, and whether the credit should be a pure invoice credit or apply to committed units. I’ve looped Aditya to own SOC2/artifacts and Monica for billing/legal.

If that direction looks OK I’ll finalize commercial language and a redline-ready term sheet. Can we set a brief call Monday?

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@lumen.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Alex — looping in the commercial details so procurement has something concrete to review. I’m proposing the following updated terms based on Ben’s note and internal approvals: 

1) Commit & Ramp
- 6M tokens/month committed, ramped over 3 billing cycles (2.5M -> 4M -> 5M -> 6M).

2) Price-Hold & Discounting
- 12-month price-hold at current blended rate contingent on an annualized 1-year minimum commitment (cannot drop below 6M/month for first 12 months).
- If you prefer a shorter firm term we can do 9-month hold + 3-month extension automatically if utilization >= 85% at month 9.

3) Credits & Billing Structure
- One-time onboarding credit: $40,000 (applied as an invoice credit over first 3 months).
- Credit bucket: $25,000 engineering credit redeemable against integration work or professional services during term (not refundable).

4) Burst & Overages
- Burst protection: up to +25% of committed tokens at a capped overage rate (50% discount to standard on-demand) for up to 30 days aggregate per 12-month period.

5) Operational commitments
- We will provide SOC 2 Type II attestation and a certificate within 5 business days after signature; Aditya is assigned to share artifacts and an index of logs.
- Dedicated billing contact and invoicing cadence: monthly with itemized token/embedding usage.

Next steps: I can prepare a term sheet and redline-ready amendment. Does Procurement want this as an amendment to the existing MSA or a short SOW addendum?

Regards,
Monica Patel
Commercial Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@lumen.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@lumen.ai>
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 11:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Monica / Ben — this looks reasonable and close to what procurement needs. A few procurement asks before we can sign: 
- We prefer amendment to existing MSA (cleaner for legal).
- Need explicit metric-based true-up language: monthly reconciliation with a 30-day notice for any commit delta >10%.
- Request clarification on the credit bucket redemption process and scope for professional services (can it be used for custom connector work?).
- Burst insurance: procurement wants an SLA-style statement that we will not be charged more than the agreed overage rate during the 30-day burst window.

If you can send a redline that incorporates these items we’ll get legal to review. Also please send the SOC 2 artifacts to procurement@lumen.ai and a DocuSign link to route for signature.

Best,
Alex

Date: Thu, 14 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Ben / Monica,

Thanks for the partnership — LumenAI is coming up on our annual renewal and usage is trending ~3.4x this year vs. last. We have a hard internal window where predictable unit economics are required to commit headcount for our new product line.

Ask: would Redwood entertain a temporary 12-month price-hold at our current blended rate if we increase our committed capacity from 2.5M tokens/month to 6M tokens/month (tiered ramp over 3 months)? We’d also like a small bucket of credits (one-time) to offset onboarding for the new team, and a short-term burst guarantee for holiday spikes.

Attached an updated SOW and current usage report. Procurement is looped for contract language. Happy to jump on a quick call this week to align next steps.

Appreciate it,
Alex
Head of Platform, LumenAI

Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Alex — thanks for the context and the SOW. Congrats on the growth — exciting product roadmap.

Quick read: we can structure a commercial that gets you the predictability you need while protecting both sides on utilization. High level proposal:
- Ramp commit to 6M tokens/month over 90 days (2.5M -> 4M -> 5M -> 6M).
- Temporary price-hold: 9 months at current blended rate, with a review at month 9 to extend to 12 based on adoption signals (usage >85% of committed).
- One-time onboarding credit: $40k applied as invoice credit, spread over first 3 months.
- Burst insurance: allow 25% over-commit burst at a pre-agreed overage rate (lower than on-demand) for up to 30 days per year.

We’ll need to confirm a few items before locking: SOC 2 evidence for procurement, data residency clause you may require, and whether the credit should be a pure invoice credit or apply to committed units. I’ve looped Aditya to own SOC2/artifacts and Monica for billing/legal.

If that direction looks OK I’ll finalize commercial language and a redline-ready term sheet. Can we set a brief call Monday?

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@lumen.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>

Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: scale & price-hold request

Alex — looping in the commercial details so procurement has something concrete to review. I’m proposing the following updated terms based on Ben’s note and internal approvals: 

1) Commit & Ramp
- 6M tokens/month committed, ramped over 3 billing cycles (2.5M -> 4M -> 5M -> 6M).

2) Price-Hold & Discounting
- 12-month price-hold at current blended rate contingent on an annualized 1-year minimum commitment (cannot drop below 6M/month for first 12 months).
- If you prefer a shorter firm term we can do 9-month hold + 3-month extension automatically if utilization >= 85% at month 9.

3) Credits & Billing Structure
- One-time onboarding credit: $40,000 (applied as an invoice credit over first 3 months).
- Credit bucket: $25,000 engineering credit redeemable against integration work or professional services during term (not refundable).

4) Burst & Overages
- Burst protection: up to +25% of committed tokens at a capped overage rate (50% discount to standard on-demand) for up to 30 days aggregate per 12-month period.

5) Operational commitments
- We will provide SOC 2 Type II attestation and a certificate within 5 business days after signature; Aditya is assigned to share artifacts and an index of logs.
- Dedicated billing contact and invoicing cadence: monthly with itemized token/embedding usage.

Next steps: I can prepare a term sheet and redline-ready amendment. Does Procurement want this as an amendment to the existing MSA or a short SOW addendum?

Regards,
Monica Patel
Commercial Operations, Redwood Inference

Monica / Ben — this looks reasonable and close to what procurement needs. A few procurement asks before we can sign: 
- We prefer amendment to existing MSA (cleaner for legal).
- Need explicit metric-based true-up language: monthly reconciliation with a 30-day notice for any commit delta >10%.
- Request clarification on the credit bucket redemption process and scope for professional services (can it be used for custom connector work?).
- Burst insurance: procurement wants an SLA-style statement that we will not be charged more than the agreed overage rate during the 30-day burst window.

If you can send a redline that incorporates these items we’ll get legal to review. Also please send the SOC 2 artifacts to procurement@lumen.ai and a DocuSign link to route for signature.

Best,
Alex

title: Demo recap + POC activation plan — LatticePoint evaluation
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Paul Khan <paul.khan@latticepoint.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Demo recap + POC activation plan — LatticePoint evaluation

Paul — thanks again for your time today. Quick recap from our demo and a proposed activation plan for the 4-week POC we discussed.

Summary of today's demo
- Walked through Redwood hosted inference for chat & embeddings.
- Showed latency numbers for 2x and 4x batching; demonstrated KV cache reuse to cut token cost.
- Discussed options for Dedicated capacity if throughput grows.

Proposed 4-week POC (high level)
1) Week 0 (alignment): finalize scope, success criteria, and security checklist.
2) Week 1 (ingest & baseline): connect sample dataset (~200k docs), run embeddings baseline and latency/load tests.
3) Week 2 (integration & prompts): integrate Redwood API into your QA pipeline; run ~50k simulated requests.
4) Week 3 (validation & regressions): evaluate quality (precision/recall on labeled prompts), run stress tests.
5) Week 4 (handoff): review results, decision meeting, next steps for production or Dedicated.

Success criteria (proposed)
- Latency: p95 < 350ms for 512-token requests under target throughput.
- Cost: per-1k-token cost within agreed threshold (compare to current baseline).
- Quality: >85% on labeled evaluation set (we'll define the eval suite together).
- Reliability: <1% error rate during test windows; successful failover to fallback model.

Owners & ask
- LatticePoint owner: Paul (technical lead) + Jamie (procurement/ops).
- Redwood owner: Ben (commercial lead), Marissa (CS lead), Lauren (solutions eng).

Attachments & links
- Attached: latticepoint-poc-checklist.xlsx, redwood-trial-config.yaml
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/1234567
- Drive: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a2b3cPOCChecklist

Next steps (please reply with):
1) Confirm the proposed success criteria or flag changes.
2) Confirm the suggested timeline or propose alternate start week.
3) Share the dataset access method (S3 link or sample export) and any security constraints.

Thanks — I'll hold a 30-minute slot next Tue/Wed for kickoff once we lock scope.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

From: Paul Khan <paul.khan@latticepoint.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@latticepoint.ai>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 18:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo recap + POC activation plan — LatticePoint evaluation

Ben — appreciate the clear plan. A few quick items from our side:

1) Success criteria: the latency target looks good but can we set p90 < 300ms and p95 < 420ms? Our traffic is spiky and p90 matters for UX.
2) Quality metric: we currently measure accuracy on 1k labeled prompts — 85% is reasonable as a starting bar; we'll aim to add another 500 prompts during Week 1.
3) Security/procurement: procurement will need SOC2 evidence and your data retention policy. Jamie is CC'd — he'll coordinate.

Dataset access: we'll provision a temporary S3 bucket and create a read-only IAM role for Redwood. Can you confirm that works and the specific IAM permissions needed?

Kickoff: Tue or Wed next week both work; prefer Wed afternoon (PDT).

-Paul

Paul Khan
Head of ML Engineering, LatticePoint
paul.khan@latticepoint.ai | +1 (206) 555-0123

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Paul Khan <paul.khan@latticepoint.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@latticepoint.ai>
Date: Sun, 31 May 2026 08:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo recap + POC activation plan — LatticePoint evaluation

Paul / team — thanks, noted. A couple clarifications and items I'll own for Redwood:

- SOC2 & retention: attaching our SOC2 summary in the Drive link; retention policy: by default logs retained 30 days for hosted customers; can extend for Dedicated. We'll surface the formal SKUs for longer retention.
- IAM permissions: read-only s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket on the bucket prefix; if you prefer a signed URL workflow we can support that too. I'll send a short checklist for the S3 role later today.
- Metrics dashboard: we'll stand up a shared Redwood Console dashboard during Week 1 for latency/cost/throughput — Marissa (CS) will monitor daily.

Action items (owners)
- Jamie (LatticePoint): create S3 bucket + IAM role OR confirm signed URL approach (by Tue EOD).
- Ben (Redwood): finalize the commercial details and send PO instructions to procurement.
- Marissa (Redwood): circulate S3 checklist and set up initial Redwood Console view; schedule recurring 30-min sync for POC standups.
- Lauren (Redwood): prepare prompt/eval template and a short runbook for embedding ingestion.

I've created a tentative kickoff invite for Wed 2026-06-03 2:00 PM PDT; Ben — please confirm and I will finalize.

Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0147

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Paul Khan <paul.khan@latticepoint.ai>, Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@latticepoint.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 31 May 2026 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo recap + POC activation plan — LatticePoint evaluation

Thanks all — Wed 6/3 2:00 PM PDT works for me. Confirming next steps on my side:

- I'll send the short contract addendum (trial terms + data handling) to procurement after I get Jamie's confirmation on the S3 approach.
- I'll also issue a draft invoice estimate for the POC compute window so procurement can start internal approvals (deal: LP-2026-0453).
- Confirming Marissa will set up the Redwood Console view and Lauren will share the prompt/eval template ahead of Week 1.

Paul — if you can share the S3 info or signed-URL preference today, we'll be ready to start on Wed.

Ben

To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Nina Alvarez <nina.alvarez@finsecure.com>

Subject: Initial ask — SOC 2 / pen-test / DPA walkthrough availability

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for the quick intro last week. Our legal and infosec teams want a combined intake meeting so we can confirm scope, evidence handoff, and the pen-test schedule — ideally before we finalize our procurement paperwork.

High-level asks:
- 60–90 minute walkthrough covering SOC 2 scope, evidence transfer (SFTP/Drive options), and a short Q&A with the pen-test vendor.
- Target week: May 31–June 4, mornings preferred PST.
- Key attendees: me (security lead), Nina (legal), and two pen-test engineers (TBD).

I attached a draft attendee/scope spreadsheet. Can you propose a slot or sequencing if a single 90-minute window is tight?

Best,
Samir
Security Lead, FinSecure
attachments: FinSecure_Scope_and_Attendees.xlsx

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@finsecure.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 27 May 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask — SOC 2 / pen-test / DPA walkthrough availability

Hi Samir — thanks for the doc, looks helpful. Quick thoughts and three concrete options below.

Option A (single compact): 90 minutes — consolidated walkthrough with our SOC 2 owner, pen-test tech brief, then a 20-minute evidence-transfer plan. Best for teams who want to minimize context switching.
Option B (split): Two back-to-back 45-minute segments — 1) control mapping & DPA overview with legal + SOC2 owner; 2) technical pen-test scoping + ops handoff. Easier if pen-test vendor needs a separate deep-dive.
Option C (staggered): 60-minute intake + 30-minute ops-only follow-up later same day — useful if your pen-test team joins asynchronously.

Availability (PST mornings) — please pick any that work and we’ll lock calendar:
- Mon 2026-05-31 09:00–10:30 (Option A)
- Tue 2026-06-01 09:00–09:45 + 10:00–10:45 (Option B)
- Wed 2026-06-02 08:30–09:30 + optional 15:00–15:30 ops follow-up (Option C)

Ben and Laura — pinging you for runway/ops headcount and whether we can support a pen-test remote bridge (SFTP + temporary user).

If none of these fit, we can propose evening windows (post-16:00 PDT) for APAC participants.

Cheers,
Chloe Martin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
attachments: SOC2_evidence_index_v2.pdf

title: EdgeEmbed POC: SDK auth, routing & 429/retry approach
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-28T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Hi Amit —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so both teams are aligned before we draft the SOW: 

What we captured from the call:
- POC goals: validate embeddings + reranker pipeline for semantic search at 50k queries/day with p95 latency < 250ms for retrieval path.
- Primary constraints: strict customer data residency (US), want per-route cost controls and predictable throttling during peak jobs.
- Integration touchpoints: SDK embed + server-side batching for bulk ingestion, realtime reranker for top-K candidate rescoring, and a lightweight tool-calling path for structured entity enrichment.

Key technical asks we promised to follow up on:
1) Auth options: we outlined API key + mTLS and mentioned support for token rotation via our platform KMS. Attached is a short "auth-options-summary.pdf" we think will help procurement.
2) Routing: recommend naming two logical routes — embeddings (gpu-opt) and reranker (cpu/gpu-mix); we can provide model aliases and automatic routing policy (cost-first vs latency-first).
3) 429 / retry behavior: propose an idempotency + request-id pattern for safe retries, and default backoff policy (expo + jitter) with respect to Retry-After headers. We included a sample header suggestion in "sample-request-ids.txt".

Proposed immediate next steps:
- Your team: confirm which auth option you prefer (API key server-to-server vs short-lived JWT) and share any procurement requirements.
- Our team: prepare POC SOW draft and a short integration checklist (SDK snippets for ingestion + client-side request ID examples).
- Kickoff: tentatively 2026-06-07 for a 1-hour tech kickoff (engineering + infra).

I've attached an initial POC scope (edgeembed-poc-scope.xlsx). Jordan and Marissa: please add anything I missed on the infra/SLAs.

Thanks,
Camila
Account Exec | Redwood Inference


From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@edgeembed.com>
Date: 2026-05-28T15:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Camila — thanks, useful summary. A couple quick clarify items from our infra team: 

- On auth: we prefer server-to-server short-lived JWTs issued by our identity provider. We need token rotation docs and rotation window expectations. Can Redwood integrate with our IDP (OIDC) or must we proxy through your KMS?

- On routing: reranker will run as an autoscaled service in our VPC; we need a deterministic way to pin reranker traffic to dedicated capacity during the POC. Is that something Redwood Dedicated can handle or do we need to add a header to route to a model alias?

- On 429s: we want a clear SLA for 429s vs throttling (is 429 only rate limit or also quota enforcement?). Also want guidance on idempotency for reranker calls (these are low QPS but must not duplicate charges/side effects).

We can do the kickoff on 6/7; please send a calendar invite.

—Amit
Sr. Engineering Manager, EdgeEmbed


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-28T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Amit —

Answers inline below, and attaching the short "auth-options-summary.pdf" for your IDP/infosec: 

- IDP / OIDC: Redwood supports OIDC for token exchange in Private deployments (we can federate to your IDP and issue short-lived platform tokens). For Hosted/Dedicated, we currently support server-to-server short-lived JWTs via our token-exchange endpoint or API keys with rotation via KMS. Which deployment mode are you evaluating for the POC? If Private/VPC, federating to your IDP is straightforward — we'll share the config doc.

- Routing to Dedicated capacity: Yes — you can pin by model-alias header (x-redwood-model-alias) or by route tag. For POC we recommend model-alias because it's explicit and works for both Hosted and Dedicated. We'll include SDK examples.

- 429 semantics: 429s are returned for rate limiting and momentary overload. Quota enforcement returns 403 with quota-identifier in the body. For idempotency: reranker calls should supply a request-id header (x-redwood-request-id) and an idempotency-key when any call could be retried and might change state. For pure scoring calls (no side effect) request-id is sufficient. See attached sample-request-ids.txt for header examples.

Next: I'll schedule the kickoff and Jordan will follow up with a short implementation checklist (SDK snippets, preferred headers, and sample backoff config).

Camila


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-29T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Hi Amit — quick technical follow-up before the kickoff: 

Implementation guidance (short bullets):
- SDK setup: we will provide language SDKs (Python/Go/Node) with a client constructor that accepts either short-lived JWTs (OIDC flow) or server API keys. Example: Client(auth=OIDC, id_token="...").
- Headers we recommend: x-redwood-model-alias, x-redwood-request-id, x-redwood-idempotency-key (optional for rerankers with side effects), and x-client-time (epoch-ms) for latency reconciliation.
- Retry policy: client default is exponential backoff with decorrelated jitter starting at 200ms, max backoff 8s, and max attempts 5. Honour Retry-After when present.
- 429 handling: treat 429 as transient — retry with backoff. If you receive repeated 429 + Retry-After > 30s, fall back to your low-cost routing (e.g., reroute to smaller model alias) or queue the request.
- Observability: include request-id in logs; Redwood will surface token-level latency/cost breakdowns — include the request-id so we can trace across both sides.

Attached: sample-request-ids.txt with header examples and the POC scope workbook Camila attached. Marcus: can you confirm we can reserve a small Dedicated pool for the reranker on the proposed dates?

Jordan
Principal Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference


From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@edgeembed.com>
Date: 2026-05-29T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Thanks Camila/Jordan — that all sounds good. We prefer OIDC federation in our VPC for the POC.

Action items on our side:
- Priya will collect procurement requirements and share the contract redline window by 6/2.
- Engineering will add the request-id header to our client and test the backoff policy you suggested.

Please send the kickoff invite for 6/7 and the POC SOW when ready. Looking forward to it.

Amit

title: Re: OptiRetail evaluation onramp — dataset exchange & limited-use prompt pack
preview:
From: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: Devon Ortiz <devon.ortiz@opti-retail.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation onramp — sample assets and MAP request

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the latency numbers looked promising and the team is excited to kick off a short evaluation. Before we schedule the technical onboarding, could you send over a short mutual action plan (MAP) and an example prompt pack we can run in our sandbox?

Scope: a 2-week onramp to validate answer quality on product search and reranking flows. We have light PII in a small subset of the corpus and will need clear usage constraints and retention guarantees.

As next steps, can you:
- Share a short MAP with milestones and owners
- Provide a sample prompt pack (few dozen seed prompts + expected acceptance criteria) under limited-use terms
- Confirm whether Redwood can host the evaluation dataset (ephemeral access) or if we need to host it and grant API access

We'd like to get started week of May 25, so a draft MAP by EOD Friday would help.

Thanks,
Maya
Head of Data Science, OptiRetail

---

Attachment: optiretail_request_spec.pdf (pdf) — short scope doc


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 12:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation onramp — sample assets and MAP request

Hi Maya — great to hear! Attaching a concise MAP and a small sample prompt pack we typically use for product-search validations. High level answers to your questions below, and the MAP has the milestone owners mapped to our team.

Quick answers:
- MAP: attached ("redwood_map_onramp_v1.pdf") — 6 checkpoints across planning, data intake, model tuning, QA, acceptance, and teardown.
- Prompt pack: attached as a compressed tarball with README and acceptance criteria ("optiretail_sample_promptpack_v1.tar.gz").
- Hosting options: Redwood can host evaluation datasets ephemeral in a sandbox we control (recommended for speed) or OptiRetail can host and provide named API keys. If you have regulatory constraints, we'll default to OptiRetail-hosted and we can run within your VPC.

Usage constraints we enforce in both modes:
- Ephemeral evaluation data is retained for a maximum of 21 days unless you request early teardown.
- No sharing outside of the named evaluation project team; we require a one-page limited-use addendum (we've attached a template).
- No commercial use beyond the evaluation scope; derivative assets must be destroyed after the onramp if not licensed.

Next steps:
1) Review the attached MAP and prompt pack.
2) Confirm hosting preference (Redwood sandbox vs. OptiRetail-hosted).
3) Sign the limited-use addendum or propose edits.

If it's helpful, Karthik can join a short 30-min intake call to finalize the timeline and access method.

Thanks — attaching both files now.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0191

Attachments: redwood_map_onramp_v1.pdf (pdf), optiretail_sample_promptpack_v1.tar.gz (tgz)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation onramp — sample assets and MAP request

Hi Maya — looping in a couple of details from the infra/ops side so you have a clear operational plan.

Access & security notes:
- If Redwood hosts the dataset: we provide ephemeral S3 storage scoped to a single evaluation job, IAM keys rotate daily, and we surface an audit log for every inference call (token-level request/response metadata). Retention: automatically destroyed 21 days post-teardown.
- If OptiRetail hosts: we'll request a named service account with IP allowlist + short-lived keys; we will not persist raw payloads in Redwood unless explicitly authorized.

Prompt pack & acceptance:
- We recommend starting with the provided seed prompts and a small set of golden transcripts (we included 30 in the pack). Acceptance criteria are per-prompt F1/accuracy thresholds and a human-review pass rate (details in README).

Operational MAP highlights (new):
- Day 0: Kickoff call + access provisioning (owners: Ava / Maya)
- Day 2–5: Data ingestion and baseline run (owner: Redwood infra / OptiRetail engineering based on hosting)
- Day 6–10: Tuning and prompt iterations (owners: solutions + OptiRetail DS)
- Day 11–14: Acceptance sprint and final QA signoff (owner: OptiRetail Product).

Open items for your procurement/security teams:
1) Limited-use addendum (one-pager) — we'll send template for review.
2) SOC2 evidence: we can provide SOC2 Type II summary and an audit-friendly data flow diagram.
3) If you require an addendum for PII handling, please share the specific retention policy and we can reflect it in the MAP.

Could you confirm who on your side will sign the limited-use addendum and whether you prefer Redwood-hosted ephemeral storage? If you want us to host, we'll provision a sandbox by Friday.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Platform Reliability | Redwood Inference

---

> 
> (See attachments)

To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Devon Ortiz <devon.ortiz@opti-retail.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2026 11:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation onramp — dataset exchange & limited-use prompt pack

Hi Ava, Karthik — thanks for the clear MAP and the prompt pack. Devon and I reviewed the limited-use addendum and have two small asks:

1) Retention: can we shorten the Redwood-hosted retention to 14 days instead of 21? Our compliance team prefers a tighter window for eval data containing customer emails.

Thanks Carlos — and thanks team. I’ve drafted a short mutual action plan (MAP) structure below and attached an editable workbook. Please review and flag any missing milestones or commercial conditions by EOD Friday. Once we align internally I’ll send to ACME for signature.

Proposed MAP lanes (high level):
- Lane A (OEM priority): sign PO within 7 business days -> NVIDIA prioritized lane -> target ship weeks 9–10 -> 6% OEM premium -> partial-shipment allowed (min 40 units)
- Lane B (Standard OEM two-tranche): sign PO -> 11–13 week window -> 40/60 split, $750 split handling fee -> tentative ship weeks assigned after PO
- Lane C (Cloud backstop): reserved nodes in us-west for 60–90 days -> immediate availability -> cost delta = $0.18/token-equivalent (approx) additional run-rate; include SLA credits equal to 5% of monthly reserved fee if capacity not available within 24 hours of scheduled burst

Milestones to capture in workbook: PO dates, invoice milestones, tentative ship week, partial-ship acceptance, cloud-reserved hold window, canary start (2026-09-15), full prod start (2026-10-15), DocuSign delivery link, HubSpot deal update.

Requests: 
- Carlos: can you confirm whether NVIDIA can accept DocuSign with the two-tranche PO terms and whether the split handling fee is negotiable at scale? Also please provide a tentative ship-week table for both Lane A and Lane B (so we can put solid dates in the MAP).
- CloudOps: please confirm the 60-day holdback cost and the SLA credit language we can present to ACME (copyable clause recommended).

I’ve attached the draft MAP: mutual_action_plan-draft.xlsx. I’ll consolidate changes and aim to route to ACME procurement next week.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head, Dedicated Capacity Planning
Redwood Inference

title: Throughput anchors & burst plan for Dedicated proposal - BlueHarbor AI
Staggered retention & trace burst credits — proposal for MARS Health
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Tran <alex@marshealth.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Observability add-on pricing — initial tier proposal

Alex —

Thanks again for the time today. Per our discussion, I attached a compact worksheet that lays out three possible retention tiers (30/90/365 days), associated tracing seat assumptions, and a separate row for the advanced dashboards add-on. A few notes up front:

- We typically price retention by primary storage + archive (hot/cold) and offer a 10% discount when customers commit to the 365-day tier for all environments.
- Tracing is priced by concurrent trace seats with a small per-seat monthly fee; customers that commit to a 12-month seat block get a price break and a pool of "burst credits" for unexpected spikes.
- Advanced dashboards are a flat monthly SKU with optional per-user seats for developer access.

I've highlighted a recommended combo for MARS based on the usage profile you shared (heavy production traces, moderate historical retention needs). See tab 'Recommended' in the attached.

Next step: would you prefer a simple committed quote (monthly) or a hybrid with paygo for retention over the committed threshold? We can model both.

Thanks,
Ben
--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0118

Attachment: MARS_Health_Retention_Tiers_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Alex Tran <alex@marshealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@marshealth.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:05:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Observability add-on pricing — initial tier proposal

Ben —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the worksheet — useful. A couple of clarifying questions from Priya and me:

1) When you say "burst credits" for tracing, how are they consumed? Are they token-based (per-span) or duration-based (concurrent minutes)? We have unpredictable surges during overnight batch runs.
2) For 365-day retention, we need guarantees around retrieval RTO for cold vs hot. What's the difference in RTO and is there any extra egress cost?

Also, pricing-wise, our procurement team prefers a blended monthly commit rather than a big annual prepay. Would a 9-month commit be an option to get a middle-ground discount?

High-level: we like the structure but want the economics to map to our bursty trace profile.

—Alex

On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Alex —
> 
> Thanks again for the time today. Per our discussion, I attached a compact worksheet...


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Tran <alex@marshealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 11:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Observability add-on pricing — initial tier proposal

Alex/Priya —

Answers inline:

1) Burst credits model: we issue burst credits as a pool of concurrent-trace-minutes. They are consumed when concurrent tracing goes above the committed seat count (i.e., when additional concurrent spans are created beyond the seat pool). Credits are metered per-minute and decrement from the pool. The workbook has an example on tab 'Burst math'. For long-running batch traces we can cap per-trace consumption so a single long trace doesn't exhaust the pool.

2) Retrieval RTO:
- Hot (30/90 tiers): typical RTO for queries under 24 hours of retention is sub-second to seconds — same serving nodes.
- Cold (365): archived chunks may have an RTO of 1–4 minutes depending on the object retrieval path; we provide an expedited path (30–60s) for a premium per-GB retrieval, otherwise it's 2–4 minutes for standard restore. No additional egress charge within the same cloud region; cross-region egress follows standard cloud provider rates.

On the commit length: we can offer a pro-rated discount schedule — 6 months: 5% off list; 9 months: 8% off; 12 months: 12% off. For 9 months we can mirror our 8% pricing with the caveat that burst credit pool size scales with the commit level.

If helpful, Marissa can draft a blended 9-month quote with the burst sizing we discussed.

Camila

--
Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com

Attachment referenced: trace-burst-credit-workbook.pdf (example calculations)


From: Priya Singh <priya.singh@marshealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Tran <alex@marshealth.com>
Date: Fri, 22 May 2026 09:31:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Observability add-on pricing — initial tier proposal

Thanks Camila — that helps. A couple more items from procurement and infra:

- Procurement: can you confirm whether the advanced dashboards SKU includes SSO provisioning (Okta) or is that an extra integration fee? We need SAML/SCIM on day one.
- Infra: if we go with 365-day retention, how does backups affect our entitlements — do backups count against retention storage or are they incremental?

Also, we're leaning toward a 9-month blended commit if you can give a firm example with numbers. Can Marissa produce a quote with: 90-day retention for dev/stage (standalone paygo), 365-day for prod (9-month blended commit), 25 trace seats committed with 10k-minute burst pool?

—Priya


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@marshealth.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 16:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Observability add-on pricing — initial tier proposal

Priya/Alex —

Attached is a 9-month blended quote PDF (also mirrored in the Drive link). Summary highlights:

- Retention: Prod (365d) committed at $2,400/mo (blended) — includes hot/cold tiering and standard restores. Dev/Stage (90d) set to paygo at $320/mo estimated (metered).
- Tracing: 25 committed seats at $45/seat/mo = $1,125/mo. Burst pool: 10,000 concurrent-minutes for the 9 months (allocated upfront), priced at $0.006/minute (total burst pool fee $60). Unused burst credits are not refundable but can be reallocated across prod/stage in the contract.
- Advanced dashboards: $1,200/mo flat for the premium visualization pack plus SCIM/SAML provisioning included (no separate fee).
- Overall blended discount applied: 8% over list (matches our 9-month policy).

Requested exceptions: procurement asked for a 30-day termination right with 60 days' notice and pro-rata for unused months; we can allow a one-time termination for compliance reasons (standard enterprise carve). Any contract-level legal exceptions will go through standard redline cadence.

If this looks good, Ben and I will send an official SOW + order form and route to your procurement for signature.

Thanks,
Marissa
